1	package chisel3.simulator
2	
3	import chisel3.{Data, RawModule, SimulationTestHarnessInterface}
4	import chisel3.experimental.inlinetest.{HasTests, SimulatedTest, TestParameters, TestResult}
5	import firrtl.options.StageUtils.dramaticMessage
6	import java.nio.file.attribute.BasicFileAttributes
7	import java.nio.file.{FileSystems, FileVisitResult, FileVisitor, Files, Path, PathMatcher, Paths}
8	import scala.collection.mutable
9	import scala.util.{Failure, Success, Try}
10	import scala.util.control.NoStackTrace
11	import svsim._
12	
13	object SimulationOutcome {
14	
15	  /** A test result, either success or failure. */
16	  sealed trait Type
17	
18	  /** Test passed. */
19	  case object Success extends Type
20	
21	  /** Test failed somehow. */
22	  sealed trait Failure extends Type
23	
24	  /** Test timed out. */
25	  case class Timeout(n: BigInt) extends Failure
26	
27	  /** Test failed with an assertion. */
28	  case class Assertion(simulatorOutput: String) extends Failure
29	
30	  /** Test signaled failure. */
31	  case object SignaledFailure extends Failure
32	}
33	
34	object Exceptions {
35	
36	  class AssertionFailed private[simulator] (message: String, val simulatorOutput: String)
37	      extends RuntimeException(
38	        dramaticMessage(
39	          header = Some("One or more assertions failed during Chiselsim simulation"),
40	          body = message
41	        )
42	      )
43	      with NoStackTrace
44	
45	  class Timeout private[simulator] (private[simulator] val timesteps: BigInt, message: String)
46	      extends RuntimeException(
47	        dramaticMessage(
48	          header = Some(s"A timeout occurred after $timesteps timesteps"),
49	          body = message
50	        )
51	      )
52	      with NoStackTrace
53	
54	  class TestFailed private[simulator]
55	      extends RuntimeException(
56	        dramaticMessage(
57	          header = Some(s"The test finished and signaled failure"),
58	          body = ""
59	        )
60	      )
61	      with NoStackTrace
62	
63	  class TestsFailed private[simulator] (
64	    message:     String,
65	    val results: Seq[chisel3.experimental.inlinetest.SimulatedTest]
66	  ) extends RuntimeException(
67	        dramaticMessage(
68	          header = Some("One or more tests failed during simulation"),
69	          body = message
70	        )
71	      )
72	      with NoStackTrace
73	}
74	
75	final object Simulator {
76	
77	  final case class BackendInvocationDigest[T](
78	    compilationStartTime: Long,
79	    compilationEndTime:   Long,
80	    outcome:              BackendInvocationOutcome[T]
81	  ) {
82	    def result = outcome match {
83	      case SimulationDigest(_, _, outcome) => outcome.get
84	      case CompilationFailed(error)        => throw error
85	    }
86	  }
87	  sealed trait BackendInvocationOutcome[T]
88	  final case class CompilationFailed[T](error: Throwable) extends BackendInvocationOutcome[T]
89	
90	  final case class SimulationDigest[T](simulationStartTime: Long, simulationEndTime: Long, outcome: Try[T])
91	      extends BackendInvocationOutcome[T]
92	}
93	
94	trait Simulator[T <: Backend] {
95	
96	  val backend: T
97	  def tag:           String
98	  def workspacePath: String
99	  def workingDirectoryPrefix = "workdir"
100	  def customSimulationWorkingDirectory:   Option[String] = None
101	  def verbose:                            Boolean = false
102	  def commonCompilationSettings:          CommonCompilationSettings
103	  def backendSpecificCompilationSettings: backend.CompilationSettings
104	
105	  /** Post process a simulation log to see if there are any backend-specific failures.
106	    *
107	    * @return None if no failures found or Some if they are
108	    */
109	  private def postProcessLog(workspace: Workspace): Option[Throwable] = {
110	    val log =
111	      Paths.get(workspace.absolutePath, s"${workspace.workingDirectoryPrefix}-${tag}", "simulation-log.txt").toFile
112	    val lines = scala.io.Source
113	      .fromFile(log)
114	      .getLines()
115	      .zipWithIndex
116	      .filter { case (line, _) => backend.assertionFailed.matches(line) }
117	      .toSeq
118	
119	    Option.when(lines.nonEmpty)(
120	      new Exceptions.AssertionFailed(
121	        simulatorOutput = lines.map(_._1).mkString("\n"),
122	        message = s"""|The following assertion failures were extracted from the log file:
123	                      |
124	                      |  lineNo  line
125	                      |  ${"-" * 76}
126	                      |${lines.map { case (line, lineNo) => f"$lineNo%8d  $line" }.mkString("\n")}
127	                      |
128	                      |For more information, see the complete log file:
129	                      |
130	                      |  ${log}""".stripMargin
131	      )
132	    )
133	  }
134	
135	  /** Simulate a Chisel module with some stimulus
136	    *
137	    * @param module a Chisel module to simulate
138	    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
139	    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
140	    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
141	    * @param body stimulus to apply to the module
142	    * @param commonSettingsModifications modifications to common compilation
143	    * settings
144	    * @param backendSettingsModifications modifications to backend (e.g.,
145	    * Verilator or VCS) compilation settings
146	    *
147	    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
148	    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
149	    */
150	  final def simulate[T <: RawModule, U](
151	    module:      => T,
152	    chiselOpts:  Array[String] = Array.empty,
153	    firtoolOpts: Array[String] = Array.empty,
154	    settings:    Settings[T] = Settings.defaultRaw[T]
155	  )(body: (SimulatedModule[T]) => U)(
156	    implicit chiselOptsModifications: ChiselOptionsModifications,
157	    firtoolOptsModifications:         FirtoolOptionsModifications,
158	    commonSettingsModifications:      svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
159	    backendSettingsModifications:     svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
160	  ): Simulator.BackendInvocationDigest[U] = {
161	    val workspace = new Workspace(path = workspacePath, workingDirectoryPrefix = workingDirectoryPrefix)
162	    workspace.reset()
163	    val instanceChoiceOpts = settings.instanceChoices.toFirtoolOptions
164	    val elaboratedModule =
165	      workspace
166	        .elaborateGeneratedModule(
167	          () => module,
168	          args = chiselOptsModifications(chiselOpts).toSeq,
169	          firtoolArgs = firtoolOptsModifications(firtoolOpts).toSeq ++ instanceChoiceOpts
170	        )
171	    _simulate(workspace, elaboratedModule, settings)(body)
172	  }
173	
174	  final def simulateTests[T <: RawModule with HasTests, U](
175	    module:           => T,
176	    includeTestGlobs: Array[String],
177	    chiselOpts:       Array[String] = Array.empty,
178	    firtoolOpts:      Array[String] = Array.empty,
179	    settings: Settings[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface] =
180	      Settings.defaultRaw[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface]
181	  )(body: (SimulatedModule[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface]) => U)(
182	    implicit chiselOptsModifications: ChiselOptionsModifications,
183	    firtoolOptsModifications:         FirtoolOptionsModifications,
184	    commonSettingsModifications:      svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
185	    backendSettingsModifications:     svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
186	  ) = {
187	    val workspace = new Workspace(path = workspacePath, workingDirectoryPrefix = workingDirectoryPrefix)
188	    workspace.reset()
189	    val filesystem = FileSystems.getDefault()
190	    val instanceChoiceOpts = settings.instanceChoices.toFirtoolOptions
191	    val results = workspace
192	      .elaborateAndMakeTestHarnessWorkspaces(
193	        () => module,
194	        includeTestGlobs = includeTestGlobs.toSeq,
195	        args = chiselOptsModifications(chiselOpts).toSeq,
196	        firtoolArgs = firtoolOptsModifications(firtoolOpts).toSeq ++ instanceChoiceOpts
197	      )
198	      .map { case (testWorkspace, elaboratedTest, elaboratedModule) =>
199	        val digest = _simulate(testWorkspace, elaboratedModule, settings)(body)
200	        // Try to unpack the result, otherwise figure out what went wrong.
201	        // TODO: push this down, i.e. all ChiselSim invocations return a SimulationOutcome
202	        val outcome: SimulationOutcome.Type =
203	          try {
204	            digest.result
205	            SimulationOutcome.Success
206	          } catch {
207	            // Simulation ended due to an aserrtion
208	            case assertion: Exceptions.AssertionFailed => SimulationOutcome.Assertion(assertion.simulatorOutput)
209	            // Simulation ended because the testharness signaled success=0
210	            case _: Exceptions.TestFailed => SimulationOutcome.SignaledFailure
211	            // Simulation timed out
212	            case to: Exceptions.Timeout => SimulationOutcome.Timeout(to.timesteps)
213	            // Simulation did not run correctly
214	            case e: Throwable => throw e
215	          }
216	        SimulatedTest(elaboratedTest, outcome)
217	      }
218	
219	    val failures = results.filter(!_.success)
220	    if (failures.nonEmpty) {
221	      val moduleName = results.head.dutName
222	      val failedTests = failures.size
223	      val passedTests = results.size - failedTests
224	      val failureMessages = failures.map { test =>
225	        s"  - ${test.testName}: ${test.result.asInstanceOf[TestResult.Failure].message}"
226	      }
227	      val aggregatedMessage =
228	        s"${moduleName} tests: ${passedTests} passed, ${failedTests} failed\nfailures: \n${failureMessages.mkString("\n")}"
229	      throw new Exceptions.TestsFailed(aggregatedMessage, results)
230	    }
231	  }
232	
233	  private def _simulate[T <: RawModule, U](
234	    workspace:        Workspace,
235	    elaboratedModule: ElaboratedModule[T],
236	    settings:         Settings[T] = Settings.defaultRaw[T]
237	  )(body: (SimulatedModule[T]) => U)(
238	    implicit chiselOptsModifications: ChiselOptionsModifications,
239	    firtoolOptsModifications:         FirtoolOptionsModifications,
240	    commonSettingsModifications:      svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
241	    backendSettingsModifications:     svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
242	  ): Simulator.BackendInvocationDigest[U] = {
243	    // Find all the directories that exist under another directory.
244	    val primarySourcesDirectories = mutable.LinkedHashSet.empty[String]
245	    class DirectoryFinder extends FileVisitor[Path] {
246	
247	      override def visitFile(file: Path, attrs: BasicFileAttributes): FileVisitResult = {
248	        FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
249	      }
250	
251	      override def preVisitDirectory(dir: Path, attrs: BasicFileAttributes): FileVisitResult = {
252	        FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
253	      }
254	
255	      override def postVisitDirectory(dir: Path, ioe: java.io.IOException): FileVisitResult = {
256	        primarySourcesDirectories += dir.toString
257	        FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
258	      }
259	
260	      override def visitFileFailed(file: Path, ioe: java.io.IOException): FileVisitResult = {
261	        throw ioe
262	      }
263	
264	    }
265	    Files.walkFileTree(Paths.get(workspace.primarySourcesPath), new DirectoryFinder)
266	
267	    // Collect a list of library names and paths from the environment. These
268	    // will be used to resolve library names provided by the user. Environment
269	    // variables take precedence over Java properties.
270	    def parseLibraryMap(value: Option[String]): Map[String, String] = {
271	      value
272	        .getOrElse("")
273	        .split(":")
274	        .filter(_.nonEmpty)
275	        .map { entry =>
276	          entry.split("=", 2) match {
277	            case Array(name, path) => name -> path
278	            case _ =>
279	              throw new IllegalArgumentException(
280	                s"Invalid link library mapping `$entry`; expected `<name>=<path>`"
281	              )
282	          }
283	        }
284	        .toMap
285	    }
286	    val libraryMapFromProp = parseLibraryMap(sys.props.get("chiselsim.libraries"))
287	    val libraryMapFromEnv = parseLibraryMap(sys.env.get("CHISELSIM_LIBS"))
288	    val libraryMap = libraryMapFromProp ++ libraryMapFromEnv
289	    val resolvedLibraryPaths = settings.libraries.map { name =>
290	      libraryMap.getOrElse(
291	        name,
292	        throw new NoSuchElementException(
293	          s"Link library `$name` not found. " +
294	            "Set the `CHISELSIM_LIBS` environment variable or the " +
295	            "`chiselsim.libraries` Java property to provide a list of " +
296	            "`<name>=<path>` mappings separated by `:`. Available libraries: " +
297	            s"[${libraryMap.keys.mkString(", ")}]"
298	        )
299	      )
300	    }
301	
302	    val commonCompilationSettingsUpdated = commonSettingsModifications(
303	      commonCompilationSettings.copy(
304	        // Append to the include directorires based on what the
305	        // workspace indicates is the path for primary sources.  This
306	        // ensures that `` `include `` directives can be resolved.
307	        includeDirs = Some(
308	          commonCompilationSettings.includeDirs.getOrElse(Seq.empty) ++ primarySourcesDirectories
309	        ),
310	        verilogPreprocessorDefines =
311	          commonCompilationSettings.verilogPreprocessorDefines ++ settings.preprocessorDefines(elaboratedModule),
312	        fileFilter = commonCompilationSettings.fileFilter
313	          .orElse(settings.verilogLayers.shouldIncludeFile(elaboratedModule)),
314	        directoryFilter = commonCompilationSettings.directoryFilter.orElse(
315	          settings.verilogLayers.shouldIncludeDirectory(elaboratedModule, workspace.primarySourcesPath)
316	        ),
317	        linkLibraryPaths = commonCompilationSettings.linkLibraryPaths ++ resolvedLibraryPaths ++ settings.libraryPaths,
318	        simulationSettings = commonCompilationSettings.simulationSettings.copy(
319	          plusArgs = commonCompilationSettings.simulationSettings.plusArgs ++ settings.plusArgs,
320	          enableWavesAtTimeZero =
321	            commonCompilationSettings.simulationSettings.enableWavesAtTimeZero || settings.enableWavesAtTimeZero
322	        )
323	      )
324	    )
325	
326	    workspace.generateAdditionalSources(timescale = commonCompilationSettingsUpdated.defaultTimescale)
327	
328	    // Compile the design.  Early exit if the compilation fails for any reason.
329	    val compilationStartTime = System.nanoTime()
330	    val simulation =
331	      try {
332	        workspace
333	          .compile(backend)(
334	            tag,
335	            commonCompilationSettingsUpdated,
336	            backendSettingsModifications(backendSpecificCompilationSettings).asInstanceOf[backend.CompilationSettings],
337	            customSimulationWorkingDirectory,
338	            verbose
339	          )
340	      } catch {
341	        case error: Throwable =>
342	          return Simulator.BackendInvocationDigest(
343	            compilationStartTime = compilationStartTime,
344	            compilationEndTime = System.nanoTime(),
345	            outcome = Simulator.CompilationFailed(error)
346	          )
347	      }
348	    val compilationEndTime = System.nanoTime()
349	
350	    // Simulate the compiled design.  After the simulation completes,
351	    // post-process the log to figure out what happened.  This post-processing
352	    // occurs in _both_ the success and failure modes for multiple reasons:
353	    //
354	    // 1. svsim returns a vague `UnexpectedEndOfMessage` on failure.
355	    // 2. svsim assumes that simulators will either exit on an assertion failure
356	    //    or can be compile-time configured to do so.  This is _not_ the case
357	    //    for VCS as VCS requires runtime configuration to do and svsim
358	    //    substitutes its own executable which ignores command line arguments.
359	    //
360	    // Note: this would be much better to handle with extensions to the FIRRTL
361	    // ABI which would abstract away these differences.
362	    val simulationOutcome = Try {
363	      simulation.runElaboratedModule(
364	        elaboratedModule = elaboratedModule,
365	        traceEnabled = commonCompilationSettingsUpdated.simulationSettings.enableWavesAtTimeZero
366	      ) { (module: SimulatedModule[T]) =>
367	        val outcome = body(module)
368	        module.completeSimulation()
369	        outcome
370	
371	      }
372	    }.transform(
373	      s /*success*/ = { case success =>
374	        postProcessLog(workspace) match {
375	          case None        => Success(success)
376	          case Some(error) => Failure(error)
377	        }
378	      },
379	      f /*failure*/ = { case originalError =>
380	        postProcessLog(workspace) match {
381	          case None           => Failure(originalError)
382	          case Some(newError) => Failure(newError)
383	        }
384	      }
385	    )
386	
387	    val simulationEndTime = System.nanoTime()
388	
389	    // Return the simulation result.
390	    Simulator.BackendInvocationDigest(
391	      compilationStartTime = compilationStartTime,
392	      compilationEndTime = compilationEndTime,
393	      outcome = Simulator.SimulationDigest(
394	        simulationStartTime = compilationEndTime,
395	        simulationEndTime = simulationEndTime,
396	        outcome = simulationOutcome
397	      )
398	    )
399	
400	  }
401	
402	}
403

1	package chisel3
2	
3	import svsim._
4	import chisel3.reflect.DataMirror
5	import chisel3.experimental.dataview.reifyIdentityView
6	import chisel3.experimental.inlinetest.{ElaboratedTest, HasTests, TestParameters}
7	import chisel3.stage.DesignAnnotation
8	import scala.collection.mutable
9	import java.nio.file.{Files, Path, Paths}
10	import firrtl.{annoSeqToSeq, seqToAnnoSeq}
11	import firrtl.AnnotationSeq
12	
13	package object simulator {
14	
15	  /** A trait that provides the minimal set of ChiselSim APIs.
16	    *
17	    * Example usage:
18	    * {{{
19	    * import chisel3.simulator.ChiselSim
20	    *
21	    * class Foo extends ChiselSim {
22	    *   /** This has access to all ChiselSim APIs like `simulate`, `peek`, and `poke`. */
23	    * }
24	    * }}}
25	    *
26	    * @see [[chisel3.simulator.scalatest.ChiselSim]]
27	    */
28	  trait ChiselSim extends ControlAPI with PeekPokeAPI with SimulatorAPI
29	
30	  /**
31	    * An opaque class that can be passed to `Simulation.run` to get access to a `SimulatedModule` in the simulation body.
32	    */
33	  final class ElaboratedModule[T] private[simulator] (
34	    private[simulator] val wrapped: T,
35	    private[simulator] val ports:   Seq[(Data, ModuleInfo.Port)],
36	    private[simulator] val layers:  Seq[chisel3.layer.Layer]
37	  ) {
38	
39	    private[chisel3] val portMap = ports.toMap
40	
41	  }
42	
43	  /**
44	    * A class that enables using a Chisel module to control an `svsim.Simulation`.
45	    */
46	  final class SimulatedModule[T] private[simulator] (
47	    private[simulator] val elaboratedModule: ElaboratedModule[T],
48	    controller:                              Simulation.Controller
49	  ) extends AnySimulatedModule(elaboratedModule.ports, controller) {
50	    def wrapped: T = elaboratedModule.wrapped
51	  }
52	  sealed class AnySimulatedModule protected (
53	    ports:          Seq[(Data, ModuleInfo.Port)],
54	    val controller: Simulation.Controller
55	  ) {
56	
57	    // -- Port Mapping
58	
59	    private val simulationPorts = ports.map { case (data, port) =>
60	      data -> controller.port(port.name)
61	    }.toMap
62	    def port(data: Data): Simulation.Port = {
63	      // TODO, we can support non identity views, but it will require changing this API to return a Seq[Port]
64	      // and packing/unpacking the BigInt literal representation.
65	      // TODO implement support for read-only.
66	      val (reified, _) = reifyIdentityView(data).getOrElse {
67	        val url = "https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/issues/new/choose"
68	        throw new Exception(
69	          s"Cannot poke $data as is a view that does not map to a single Data. " +
70	            s"Please file an issue at $url requesting support for this use case."
71	        )
72	      }
73	      simulationPorts(reified)
74	    }
75	
76	    // -- Peek/Poke API Support
77	
78	    // When using the low-level API, the user must explicitly call `controller.completeInFlightCommands()` to ensure that all commands are executed. When using a higher-level API like peek/poke, we handle this automatically.
79	    private var shouldCompleteInFlightCommands: Boolean = false
80	    private[simulator] def completeSimulation() = {
81	      if (shouldCompleteInFlightCommands) {
82	        shouldCompleteInFlightCommands = false
83	        controller.completeInFlightCommands()
84	      }
85	    }
86	
87	    // The peek/poke API implicitly evaluates on the first peek after one or more pokes. This is _only_ for peek/poke and using `controller` directly will not provide this behavior.
88	    private var evaluateBeforeNextPeek: Boolean = false
89	    private[simulator] def willEvaluate() = {
90	      evaluateBeforeNextPeek = false
91	    }
92	    private[simulator] def willPoke() = {
93	      shouldCompleteInFlightCommands = true
94	      evaluateBeforeNextPeek = true
95	    }
96	    private[simulator] def willPeek() = {
97	      shouldCompleteInFlightCommands = true
98	      if (evaluateBeforeNextPeek) {
99	        willEvaluate()
100	        controller.run(0)
101	      }
102	    }
103	  }
104	  private[simulator] object AnySimulatedModule {
105	    private val dynamicVariable = new scala.util.DynamicVariable[Option[AnySimulatedModule]](None)
106	    def withValue[T](module: AnySimulatedModule)(body: => T): T = {
107	      require(dynamicVariable.value.isEmpty, "Nested simulations are not supported.")
108	      dynamicVariable.withValue(Some(module))(body)
109	    }
110	    def current: AnySimulatedModule = dynamicVariable.value.get
111	  }
112	
113	  implicit class ChiselSimulation(simulation: Simulation) {
114	    def runElaboratedModule[T, U](
115	      elaboratedModule:              ElaboratedModule[T],
116	      conservativeCommandResolution: Boolean = false,
117	      verbose:                       Boolean = false,
118	      traceEnabled:                  Boolean = false,
119	      executionScriptLimit:          Option[Int] = None
120	    )(body: SimulatedModule[T] => U): U = {
121	      simulation.run(conservativeCommandResolution, verbose, traceEnabled, executionScriptLimit) { controller =>
122	        val module = new SimulatedModule(elaboratedModule, controller)
123	        AnySimulatedModule.withValue(module) {
124	          body(module)
125	        }
126	      }
127	    }
128	  }
129	
130	  implicit class ChiselWorkspace(workspace: Workspace) {
131	    def elaborateGeneratedModule[T <: RawModule](
132	      generateModule: () => T,
133	      args:           Seq[String] = Seq.empty,
134	      firtoolArgs:    Seq[String] = Seq.empty
135	    ): ElaboratedModule[T] = {
136	      val generated = generateWorkspaceSources(generateModule, args, firtoolArgs)
137	      val ports = getModuleInfoPorts(generated.dut)
138	      val moduleInfo = initializeModuleInfo(generated.dut, ports.map(_._2))
139	      val layers = generated.outputAnnotations.collectFirst { case DesignAnnotation(_, layers) => layers }.get
140	      new ElaboratedModule(generated.dut, ports, layers)
141	    }
142	
143	    def elaborateAndMakeTestHarnessWorkspaces[T <: RawModule with HasTests](
144	      generateModule:   () => T,
145	      includeTestGlobs: Seq[String],
146	      args:             Seq[String] = Seq.empty,
147	      firtoolArgs:      Seq[String] = Seq.empty
148	    ): Seq[(Workspace, ElaboratedTest[T], ElaboratedModule[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface])] = {
149	      val updatedArgs = args ++ includeTestGlobs.map("--include-tests-name=" + _)
150	      val generated = generateWorkspaceSources(generateModule, updatedArgs, firtoolArgs)
151	      generated.testHarnesses.map { case elaboratedTest =>
152	        val testWorkspace = workspace.shallowCopy(workspace.absolutePath + "/tests/" + elaboratedTest.testName)
153	        val ports = getModuleInfoPorts(elaboratedTest.testHarness)
154	        val moduleInfo = testWorkspace.initializeModuleInfo(elaboratedTest.testHarness, ports.map(_._2))
155	        val layers = generated.outputAnnotations.collectFirst { case DesignAnnotation(_, layers) => layers }.get
156	        (
157	          testWorkspace,
158	          elaboratedTest,
159	          new ElaboratedModule(
160	            elaboratedTest.testHarness.asInstanceOf[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface],
161	            ports,
162	            layers
163	          )
164	        )
165	      }
166	    }
167	
168	    case class GeneratedWorkspaceInfo[T <: RawModule](
169	      dut:               T,
170	      testHarnesses:     Seq[ElaboratedTest[T]],
171	      outputAnnotations: AnnotationSeq
172	    )
173	
174	    /** Use CIRCT to generate SystemVerilog sources, and potentially additional artifacts */
175	    def generateWorkspaceSources[T <: RawModule](
176	      generateModule: () => T,
177	      args:           Seq[String],
178	      firtoolArgs:    Seq[String]
179	    ): GeneratedWorkspaceInfo[T] = {
180	      var someDut:           Option[() => T] = None
181	      var someTestHarnesses: Option[() => Seq[ElaboratedTest[T]]] = None
182	      val chiselArgs = Array("--target", "systemverilog", "--split-verilog") ++ args
183	      val outputAnnotations = (new circt.stage.ChiselStage).execute(
184	        chiselArgs,
185	        Seq(
186	          chisel3.stage.ChiselGeneratorAnnotation { () =>
187	            val dut = generateModule()
188	            someDut = Some(() => dut)
189	            someTestHarnesses = Some(() =>
190	              dut match {
191	                case dut: HasTests => dut.getElaboratedTests.map(_.asInstanceOf[ElaboratedTest[T]])
192	                case _ => Nil
193	              }
194	            )
195	            dut
196	          },
197	          circt.stage.FirtoolOption("-disable-annotation-unknown"),
198	          firrtl.options.TargetDirAnnotation(workspace.supportArtifactsPath)
199	        ) ++ firtoolArgs.map(circt.stage.FirtoolOption(_))
200	      )
201	
202	      // Move the files indicated by a filelist.  No-op if the file has already
203	      // been moved.
204	      val movedFiles = mutable.HashSet.empty[Path]
205	      val supportArtifactsPath = Paths.get(workspace.supportArtifactsPath)
206	      def moveFile(file: Path): Unit = {
207	        // Convert the files to an absolute version and a relative version.
208	        val (_abs, rel) = file match {
209	          case file if file.startsWith(supportArtifactsPath) =>
210	            (file, file.subpath(supportArtifactsPath.getNameCount(), file.getNameCount()))
211	          case file => (supportArtifactsPath.resolve(file), file)
212	        }
213	        // Normalize the absolute path so it can be checked if it has already
214	        // been moved.
215	        val abs = _abs.normalize()
216	        // Move the file into primarySourcesPath if it has not already been moved.
217	        (abs, rel) match {
218	          case (abs, _) if movedFiles.contains(abs) =>
219	          case (abs, rel) =>
220	            val dest = Paths.get(workspace.primarySourcesPath).resolve(rel)
221	            dest.getParent.toFile.mkdirs
222	            Files.move(abs, dest)
223	            movedFiles += abs
224	        }
225	      }
226	
227	      // Move all files from the build flow that have file extensions that
228	      // should be included:
229	      //   - Verilog files: .v, .sv, .vh, .svh
230	      //   - C++ files: .cc, .cpp, .h
231	      val includedExtensions = Set("v", "sv", "vh", "svh", "cc", "cpp", "h")
232	      Files
233	        .walk(supportArtifactsPath)
234	        .filter(_.toFile.isFile)
235	        .filter { f =>
236	          val filename = f.getFileName.toString
237	          val dotIndex = filename.lastIndexOf('.')
238	          dotIndex >= 0 && includedExtensions.contains(filename.substring(dotIndex + 1))
239	        }
240	        .forEach(moveFile)
241	
242	      GeneratedWorkspaceInfo(
243	        someDut.get(),
244	        someTestHarnesses.get(),
245	        outputAnnotations
246	      )
247	    }
248	
249	    def getModuleInfoPorts(dut: RawModule): Seq[(Data, ModuleInfo.Port)] = {
250	
251	      /**
252	          * We infer the names of various ports since we don't currently have a good alternative when using MFC. We hope to replace this once we get better support from CIRCT.
253	          */
254	      def leafPorts(node: Data, name: String): Seq[(Data, ModuleInfo.Port)] = {
255	        node match {
256	          case record: Record => {
257	            record.elements.toSeq.flatMap { case (fieldName, field) =>
258	              leafPorts(field, s"${name}_${fieldName}")
259	            }
260	          }
261	          case vec: Vec[_] => {
262	            vec.zipWithIndex.flatMap { case (element, index) =>
263	              leafPorts(element, s"${name}_${index}")
264	            }
265	          }
266	          case element: Element =>
267	            // Return the port only if the width is positive (firtool will optimized it out from the *.sv primary source)
268	            if (element.widthKnown && element.getWidth > 0) {
269	              DataMirror.directionOf(element) match {
270	                case ActualDirection.Input =>
271	                  Seq((element, ModuleInfo.Port(name, isGettable = true, isSettable = true)))
272	                case ActualDirection.Output => Seq((element, ModuleInfo.Port(name, isGettable = true)))
273	                case _                      => Seq()
274	              }
275	            } else {
276	              Seq()
277	            }
278	        }
279	      }
280	      // Chisel ports can be Data or Property, but there is no ABI for Property ports, so we only return Data.
281	      DataMirror.modulePorts(dut).flatMap {
282	        case (name, data: Data) => leafPorts(data, name)
283	        case _                  => Nil
284	      }
285	    }
286	
287	    def initializeModuleInfo(dut: RawModule, ports: Seq[ModuleInfo.Port]): Unit = {
288	      val info = ModuleInfo(
289	        name = dut.name,
290	        ports = ports
291	      )
292	      workspace.elaborate(info)
293	    }
294	  }
295	}

content.content:
1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	
3	package svsim.verilator
4	
5	import svsim._
6	
7	import scala.collection.mutable
8	import scala.sys.process._
9	
10	object Backend {
11	  object CompilationSettings {
12	
13	    object TraceKind {
14	
15	      sealed trait Type {
16	        private[Backend] def toCompileFlags: Seq[String]
17	      }
18	
19	      /** VCD tracing */
20	      case object Vcd extends Type {
21	        final def toCompileFlags = Seq("--trace")
22	      }
23	
24	      /** FST tracing
25	        *
26	        * @param traceThreads Enable FST waveform creation using `traceThreads` separate threads
27	        */
28	      case class Fst(traceThreads: Option[Int] = None) extends Type {
29	        final def toCompileFlags: Seq[String] =
30	          Seq("--trace-fst") ++ traceThreads.map(n => Seq("--trace-threads", n.toString)).toSeq.flatten
31	      }
32	    }
33	
34	    /** Trace style options for verilator
35	      *
36	      * @param kind The format of the trace to generate, e.g., VCD or FST
37	      * @param traceUnderscore Whether to trace signals with names starting with an underscore
38	      * @param traceStructs Whether to trace structs
39	      * @param traceParams Whether to trace parameters
40	      * @param maxWidth The maximum bit width for tracing
41	      * @param maxArraySize The maximum array depth for tracing
42	      * @param traceDepth The maximum depth of tracing
43	      */
44	    case class TraceStyle(
45	      kind:            TraceKind.Type,
46	      traceUnderscore: Boolean = false,
47	      traceStructs:    Boolean = true,
48	      traceParams:     Boolean = false,
49	      maxWidth:        Option[Int] = None,
50	      maxArraySize:    Option[Int] = None,
51	      traceDepth:      Option[Int] = None
52	    ) {
53	      def toCompileFlags: Seq[String] = kind.toCompileFlags ++
54	        Option.when(traceUnderscore)("--trace-underscore") ++
55	        Option.when(traceStructs)("--trace-structs") ++
56	        Option.when(traceParams)("--trace-params") ++
57	        (
58	          maxArraySize.map(n => Seq(s"--trace-max-array", n.toString)) ++
59	            maxWidth.map(n => Seq("--trace-max-width", n.toString)) ++
60	            traceDepth.map(n => Seq("--trace-depth", n.toString))
61	        ).flatten
62	    }
63	
64	    /** Settings for controlling Verilator coverage instrumentation.
65	      *
66	      * These options map to Verilator coverage flags:
67	      *
68	      *   - `--coverage-line`
69	      *   - `--coverage-toggle`
70	      *   - `--coverage-user`
71	      *
72	      * If all coverage types are enabled, `--coverage` is used.
73	      *
74	      * @param line enable line coverage instrumentation
75	      * @param toggle enable toggle coverage instrumentation
76	      * @param user enable user coverage instrumentation for `cover` statements
77	      */
78	    final class CoverageSettings(
79	      val line:   Boolean = false,
80	      val toggle: Boolean = false,
81	      val user:   Boolean = false
82	    ) {
83	      private[Backend] def any: Boolean = line || toggle || user
84	
85	      private[Backend] def toCompileFlags: Seq[String] = {
86	        if (line && toggle && user) {
87	          Seq("--coverage")
88	        } else {
89	          Seq(
90	            Option.when(line)("--coverage-line"),
91	            Option.when(toggle)("--coverage-toggle"),
92	            Option.when(user)("--coverage-user")
93	          ).flatten
94	        }
95	      }
96	    }
97	
98	    object Timing {
99	      sealed trait Type
100	      case object TimingEnabled extends Type
101	      case object TimingDisabled extends Type
102	    }
103	
104	    /** Control job parallelism in verilator */
105	    object Parallelism {
106	      sealed trait Type {
107	        def toCompileFlags: Seq[String]
108	      }
109	
110	      /** Apply uniform parallelism to Verilation.  This maps to `-j`. */
111	      class Uniform private (num: Int) extends Type {
112	        override def toCompileFlags = Seq("-j", num.toString)
113	
114	        private def copy(num: Int): Uniform = new Uniform(num = num)
115	
116	        def withNum(num: Int): Uniform = copy(num = num)
117	      }
118	
119	      object Uniform {
120	        def default: Uniform = new Uniform(num = 0)
121	      }
122	
123	      /** Apply non-uniform parallelism to Verilation.  This allows control of
124	        * `--build-jobs` and `--verilate-jobs` separately.
125	        */
126	      class Different private (build: Option[Int], verilate: Option[Int]) extends Type {
127	        override def toCompileFlags: Seq[String] = {
128	          val buildJobs:    Seq[String] = build.map(num => Seq("--build-jobs", num.toString)).toSeq.flatten
129	          val verilateJobs: Seq[String] = verilate.map(num => Seq("--verilate-jobs", num.toString)).toSeq.flatten
130	          buildJobs ++ verilateJobs
131	        }
132	
133	        private def copy(build: Option[Int] = this.build, verilate: Option[Int] = this.verilate): Different =
134	          new Different(
135	            build = build,
136	            verilate = verilate
137	          )
138	
139	        def withBuild(build: Option[Int]): Different = copy(build = build)
140	
141	        def withVerilate(verilate: Option[Int]): Different = copy(verilate = verilate)
142	      }
143	
144	      object Different {
145	        def default = new Different(build = None, verilate = None)
146	      }
147	    }
148	
149	    @deprecated("use 'CompilationSettings.default' and 'with<name>' helpers", "Chisel 7.1.0")
150	    def apply(
151	      traceStyle:                 Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle] = None,
152	      outputSplit:                Option[Int] = None,
153	      outputSplitCFuncs:          Option[Int] = None,
154	      disabledWarnings:           Seq[String] = Seq(),
155	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean = false,
156	      enableAllAssertions:        Boolean = false,
157	      timing:                     Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type] = None
158	    ): CompilationSettings = CompilationSettings(
159	      traceStyle,
160	      outputSplit,
161	      outputSplitCFuncs,
162	      disabledWarnings,
163	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
164	      enableAllAssertions,
165	      timing,
166	      Some(CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Uniform.default),
167	      new CompilationSettings.CoverageSettings()
168	    )
169	
170	    @deprecated("avoid use of unapply", "Chisel 7.1.0")
171	    def unapply(compilationSettings: CompilationSettings): Option[
172	      (
173	        Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle],
174	        Option[Int],
175	        Option[Int],
176	        Seq[String],
177	        Boolean,
178	        Boolean,
179	        Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type]
180	      )
181	    ] = Some(
182	      (
183	        compilationSettings.traceStyle,
184	        compilationSettings.outputSplit,
185	        compilationSettings.outputSplitCFuncs,
186	        compilationSettings.disabledWarnings,
187	        compilationSettings.disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
188	        compilationSettings.enableAllAssertions,
189	        compilationSettings.timing
190	      )
191	    )
192	
193	    def default: CompilationSettings = new CompilationSettings(
194	      traceStyle = None,
195	      outputSplit = None,
196	      outputSplitCFuncs = None,
197	      disabledWarnings = Seq(),
198	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings = false,
199	      enableAllAssertions = false,
200	      timing = None,
201	      parallelism = Some(CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Uniform.default),
202	      coverageSettings = new CompilationSettings.CoverageSettings()
203	    )
204	
205	  }
206	
207	  case class CompilationSettings private (
208	    traceStyle:                 Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle],
209	    outputSplit:                Option[Int],
210	    outputSplitCFuncs:          Option[Int],
211	    disabledWarnings:           Seq[String],
212	    disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean,
213	    enableAllAssertions:        Boolean,
214	    timing:                     Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type],
215	    parallelism:                Option[CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Type],
216	    coverageSettings:           CompilationSettings.CoverageSettings
217	  ) extends svsim.Backend.Settings {
218	
219	    @deprecated("use 'CompilationSettings.default' and 'with<name>' helpers", "Chisel 7.1.0")
220	    def this(
221	      traceStyle:                 Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle] = None,
222	      outputSplit:                Option[Int] = None,
223	      outputSplitCFuncs:          Option[Int] = None,
224	      disabledWarnings:           Seq[String] = Seq(),
225	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean = false,
226	      enableAllAssertions:        Boolean = false,
227	      timing:                     Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type] = None
228	    ) = this(
229	      traceStyle,
230	      outputSplit,
231	      outputSplitCFuncs,
232	      disabledWarnings,
233	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
234	      enableAllAssertions,
235	      timing,
236	      Some(CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Uniform.default),
237	      new CompilationSettings.CoverageSettings()
238	    )
239	
240	    private def copyInternal(
241	      traceStyle:                 Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle] = this.traceStyle,
242	      outputSplit:                Option[Int] = this.outputSplit,
243	      outputSplitCFuncs:          Option[Int] = this.outputSplitCFuncs,
244	      disabledWarnings:           Seq[String] = this.disabledWarnings,
245	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean = this.disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
246	      enableAllAssertions:        Boolean = this.enableAllAssertions,
247	      timing:                     Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type] = this.timing,
248	      parallelism:                Option[CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Type] = this.parallelism,
249	      coverageSettings:           CompilationSettings.CoverageSettings = this.coverageSettings
250	    ): CompilationSettings = CompilationSettings(
251	      traceStyle = traceStyle,
252	      outputSplit = outputSplit,
253	      outputSplitCFuncs = outputSplitCFuncs,
254	      disabledWarnings = disabledWarnings,
255	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings = disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
256	      enableAllAssertions = enableAllAssertions,
257	      timing = timing,
258	      parallelism = parallelism,
259	      coverageSettings = coverageSettings
260	    )
261	
262	    // Keep this signature stable for binary compatibility with prior releases.
263	    def _copy(
264	      traceStyle:                 Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle] = this.traceStyle,
265	      outputSplit:                Option[Int] = this.outputSplit,
266	      outputSplitCFuncs:          Option[Int] = this.outputSplitCFuncs,
267	      disabledWarnings:           Seq[String] = this.disabledWarnings,
268	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean = this.disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
269	      enableAllAssertions:        Boolean = this.enableAllAssertions,
270	      timing:                     Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type] = this.timing,
271	      parallelism:                Option[CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Type] = this.parallelism
272	    ): CompilationSettings = copyInternal(
273	      traceStyle = traceStyle,
274	      outputSplit = outputSplit,
275	      outputSplitCFuncs = outputSplitCFuncs,
276	      disabledWarnings = disabledWarnings,
277	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings = disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
278	      enableAllAssertions = enableAllAssertions,
279	      timing = timing,
280	      parallelism = parallelism,
281	      coverageSettings = this.coverageSettings
282	    )
283	
284	    @deprecated("don't use the copy method, use 'with<name>' single setters", "Chisel 7.1.0")
285	    def copy(
286	      traceStyle:                 Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle] = this.traceStyle,
287	      outputSplit:                Option[Int] = this.outputSplit,
288	      outputSplitCFuncs:          Option[Int] = this.outputSplitCFuncs,
289	      disabledWarnings:           Seq[String] = this.disabledWarnings,
290	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean = this.disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
291	      enableAllAssertions:        Boolean = this.enableAllAssertions,
292	      timing:                     Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type] = this.timing
293	    ): CompilationSettings = _copy(
294	      traceStyle = traceStyle,
295	      outputSplit = outputSplit,
296	      outputSplitCFuncs = outputSplitCFuncs,
297	      disabledWarnings = disabledWarnings,
298	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings = disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
299	      enableAllAssertions = enableAllAssertions,
300	      timing = timing,
301	      parallelism = this.parallelism
302	    )
303	
304	    // Suppress generation of private copy with default arguments by Scala 3
305	    private def copy(
306	      traceStyle:                 Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle],
307	      outputSplit:                Option[Int],
308	      outputSplitCFuncs:          Option[Int],
309	      disabledWarnings:           Seq[String],
310	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean,
311	      enableAllAssertions:        Boolean,
312	      timing:                     Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type],
313	      parallelism:                Option[CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Type],
314	      coverageSettings:           CompilationSettings.CoverageSettings
315	    ): CompilationSettings = copyInternal(
316	      traceStyle = traceStyle,
317	      outputSplit = outputSplit,
318	      outputSplitCFuncs = outputSplitCFuncs,
319	      disabledWarnings = disabledWarnings,
320	      disableFatalExitOnWarnings = disableFatalExitOnWarnings,
321	      enableAllAssertions = enableAllAssertions,
322	      timing = timing,
323	      parallelism = Some(CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Uniform.default),
324	      coverageSettings = coverageSettings
325	    )
326	
327	    def withTraceStyle(traceStyle: Option[CompilationSettings.TraceStyle]) = _copy(traceStyle = traceStyle)
328	
329	    def withOutputSplit(outputSplit: Option[Int]) = _copy(outputSplit = outputSplit)
330	
331	    def withOutputSplitCFuncs(outputSplitCFuncs: Option[Int]) = _copy(outputSplitCFuncs = outputSplitCFuncs)
332	
333	    def withDisabledWarnings(disabledWarnings: Seq[String]) = _copy(disabledWarnings = disabledWarnings)
334	
335	    def withDisableFatalExitOnWarnings(disableFatalExitOnWarnings: Boolean) =
336	      _copy(disableFatalExitOnWarnings = disableFatalExitOnWarnings)
337	
338	    def withEnableAllAssertions(enableAllAssertions: Boolean) = _copy(enableAllAssertions = enableAllAssertions)
339	
340	    /** Configure coverage instrumentation for Verilator.
341	      *
342	      * @param coverageSettings coverage kinds to enable during compilation
343	      */
344	    def withCoverageSettings(coverageSettings: CompilationSettings.CoverageSettings) =
345	      copyInternal(coverageSettings = coverageSettings)
346	
347	    def withTiming(timing: Option[CompilationSettings.Timing.Type]) = _copy(timing = timing)
348	
349	    def withParallelism(parallelism: Option[CompilationSettings.Parallelism.Type]) = _copy(parallelism = parallelism)
350	  }
351	
352	  def initializeFromProcessEnvironment() = {
353	    val output = mutable.ArrayBuffer.empty[String]
354	    val exitCode = List("which", "verilator").!(ProcessLogger(output += _))
355	    if (exitCode != 0) {
356	      throw new Exception(s"verilator not found on the PATH!\n${output.mkString("\n")}")
357	    }
358	    val executablePath = output.head.trim
359	    new Backend(executablePath = executablePath)
360	  }
361	}
362	final class Backend(executablePath: String) extends svsim.Backend {
363	  type CompilationSettings = Backend.CompilationSettings
364	
365	  def generateParameters(
366	    outputBinaryName:        String,
367	    topModuleName:           String,
368	    additionalHeaderPaths:   Seq[String],
369	    commonSettings:          CommonCompilationSettings,
370	    backendSpecificSettings: CompilationSettings
371	  ): svsim.Backend.Parameters = {
372	    import Backend.CompilationSettings._
373	    import CommonCompilationSettings._
374	    //format: off
375	
376	    val args = mutable.ArrayBuffer.empty[String]
377	    def addArg(xs: Iterable[String]): Unit = args ++= xs
378	    def addArgParts(flag: String, parts: Iterable[String]): Unit = {
379	      val v = parts.iterator.mkString(" ")
380	      if (v.nonEmpty) addArg(Seq(flag, v))
381	    }
382	
383	    // Base verilator invocation
384	    addArg(Seq(
385	      "--cc",
386	      "--exe",
387	      "--build",
388	      "-o", s"../$outputBinaryName",
389	      "--top-module", topModuleName,
390	      "--Mdir", "verilated-sources",
391	      "--assert"
392	    ))
393	
394	    backendSpecificSettings.parallelism match {
395	      case Some(parallelism) => addArg(parallelism.toCompileFlags)
396	      case None => ()
397	    }
398	
399	    commonSettings.libraryExtensions.foreach { extensions =>
400	      addArg(Seq((Seq("+libext") ++ extensions).mkString("+")))
401	    }
402	
403	    commonSettings.libraryPaths.foreach { paths =>
404	      paths.foreach(p => addArg(Seq("-y", p)))
405	    }
406	
407	    commonSettings.linkLibraryPaths.foreach(lib => addArg(Seq(lib)))
408	
409	    commonSettings.includeDirs.foreach { dirs =>
410	      addArg(dirs.map(dir => s"+incdir+$dir"))
411	    }
412	
413	    commonSettings.defaultTimescale.foreach { value =>
414	      addArg(Seq("--timescale", value.toString))
415	    }
416	
417	    backendSpecificSettings.traceStyle.foreach { ts => addArg(ts.toCompileFlags) }
418	
419	    addArg(backendSpecificSettings.coverageSettings.toCompileFlags)
420	
421	    backendSpecificSettings.timing match {
422	      case Some(Timing.TimingEnabled)  => addArg(Seq("--timing"))
423	      case Some(Timing.TimingDisabled) => addArg(Seq("--no-timing"))
424	      case None                        =>
425	    }
426	
427	    if (backendSpecificSettings.disableFatalExitOnWarnings) addArg(Seq("-Wno-fatal"))
428	    if (backendSpecificSettings.enableAllAssertions) addArg(Seq("--assert"))
429	    addArg(backendSpecificSettings.disabledWarnings.map("-Wno-" + _))
430	
431	    commonSettings.optimizationStyle match {
432	      case OptimizationStyle.Default =>
433	      case OptimizationStyle.OptimizeForCompilationSpeed => addArg(Seq("-O1"))
434	      case OptimizationStyle.OptimizeForSimulationSpeed => addArg(Seq("-O3", "--x-assign", "fast", "--x-initial", "fast"))
435	    }
436	
437	    backendSpecificSettings.outputSplit.foreach      { n => addArg(Seq("--output-split", n.toString)) }
438	    backendSpecificSettings.outputSplitCFuncs.foreach{ n => addArg(Seq("--output-split-cfuncs", n.toString)) }
439	
440	    val makeflagsParts: Seq[String] = commonSettings.availableParallelism match {
441	      case AvailableParallelism.Default       => Seq()
442	      case AvailableParallelism.UpTo(value)   => Seq("-j", value.toString)
443	    }
444	    addArgParts("-MAKEFLAGS", makeflagsParts)
445	
446	    val cflagsParts: Seq[String] = {
447	      val opt = commonSettings.optimizationStyle match {
448	        case OptimizationStyle.Default => Seq()
449	        case OptimizationStyle.OptimizeForCompilationSpeed => Seq("-O1")
450	        case OptimizationStyle.OptimizeForSimulationSpeed   => Seq("-O3", "-march=native", "-mtune=native")
451	      }
452	      val std = Seq("-std=c++17")
453	      val inc = additionalHeaderPaths.map(path => s"-I$path")
454	      val defs = Seq(s"-D${svsim.Backend.HarnessCompilationFlags.enableVerilatorSupport}") ++
455	        Option.when(backendSpecificSettings.traceStyle.nonEmpty)(
456	          s"-D${svsim.Backend.HarnessCompilationFlags.enableVerilatorTrace}"
457	        ) ++
458	        Option.when(backendSpecificSettings.coverageSettings.any)(
459	          s"-D${svsim.Backend.HarnessCompilationFlags.enableVerilatorCoverage}"
460	        )
461	      opt ++ std ++ inc ++ defs
462	    }
463	    addArgParts("-CFLAGS", cflagsParts)
464	
465	    val defineFlags: Seq[String] = {
466	      val base = commonSettings.verilogPreprocessorDefines
467	      val traceExtra = backendSpecificSettings.traceStyle match {
468	        case None => Seq()
469	        case Some(Backend.CompilationSettings.TraceStyle(Backend.CompilationSettings.TraceKind.Vcd, _, _, _, _, _, _)) =>
470	          Seq(VerilogPreprocessorDefine(svsim.Backend.HarnessCompilationFlags.enableVcdTracingSupport))
471	        case Some(Backend.CompilationSettings.TraceStyle(Backend.CompilationSettings.TraceKind.Fst(_), _, _, _, _, _, _)) =>
472	          Seq(VerilogPreprocessorDefine(svsim.Backend.HarnessCompilationFlags.enableFstTracingSupport))
473	      }
474	      (base ++ traceExtra).map(_.toCommandlineArgument(this))
475	    }
476	    addArg(defineFlags)
477	
478	    svsim.Backend.Parameters(
479	      compilerPath = executablePath,
480	      compilerInvocation = svsim.Backend.Parameters.Invocation(
481	        arguments = args.toSeq,
482	        environment = Seq()
483	      ),
484	      simulationInvocation = svsim.Backend.Parameters.Invocation(
485	        commonSettings.simulationSettings.plusArgs.map(_.simulatorFlags),
486	        Seq()
487	      )
488	    )
489	    //format: on
490	  }
491	
492	  override def escapeDefine(string: String): String = string
493	
494	  override val assertionFailed = "^.*Assertion failed in.*".r
495	
496	}
497

7	trait CommonSettingsModifications extends (CommonCompilationSettings => CommonCompilationSettings)

9	object CommonSettingsModifications {
10	
11	  implicit def unmodified: CommonSettingsModifications = identity(_)
12	
13	}
14	
15	// -- Compilation Settings
16	
17	/** Backend-independent simulation runtime settings
18	  *
19	  * @note Use [[CommonSimulationSettings$]] methods to create objects of this
20	  * class.
21	  */
22	final class CommonSimulationSettings private[svsim] (
23	  val plusArgs:              Seq[PlusArg],
24	  val enableWavesAtTimeZero: Boolean,
25	  val traceFileStem:         String
26	) {
27	
28	  require(traceFileStem.nonEmpty, "traceFileStem must be a non-empty String")
29	
30	  /** Return a copy of this [[CommonSimulationSettings]] with some fields
31	    * modified.
32	    */
33	  def copy(
34	    plusArgs:              Seq[PlusArg] = plusArgs,
35	    enableWavesAtTimeZero: Boolean = enableWavesAtTimeZero,
36	    traceFileStem:         String = traceFileStem
37	  ) = new CommonSimulationSettings(

40	    traceFileStem = traceFileStem

42	}

44	object CommonSimulationSettings {
45	
46	  /** Return a [[CommonSimulationSettings]] with default values.
47	    *
48	    * @param plusArgs Verilog value or test plusargs to set at simulation
49	    * runtime
50	    */
51	  def default = new CommonSimulationSettings(
52	    plusArgs = Seq.empty,
53	    enableWavesAtTimeZero = false,
54	    traceFileStem = "trace"
55	  )
56	
57	}

59	/** Settings supported by all svsim backends.
60	  */
61	case class CommonCompilationSettings(
62	  verilogPreprocessorDefines: Seq[CommonCompilationSettings.VerilogPreprocessorDefine] = Seq(),
63	  optimizationStyle: CommonCompilationSettings.OptimizationStyle = CommonCompilationSettings.OptimizationStyle.Default,
64	  availableParallelism: CommonCompilationSettings.AvailableParallelism =
65	    CommonCompilationSettings.AvailableParallelism.Default,
66	  defaultTimescale:   Option[CommonCompilationSettings.Timescale] = Some(CommonCompilationSettings.Timescale.default),
67	  libraryExtensions:  Option[Seq[String]] = None,
68	  libraryPaths:       Option[Seq[String]] = None,
69	  includeDirs:        Option[Seq[String]] = None,
70	  fileFilter:         PartialFunction[File, Boolean] = PartialFunction.empty,
71	  directoryFilter:    PartialFunction[File, Boolean] = PartialFunction.empty,
72	  simulationSettings: CommonSimulationSettings = CommonSimulationSettings.default,
73	  // @unroll is required to maintain binary compatibility
74	  @unroll linkLibraryPaths: Seq[String] = Seq.empty
75	)
76	object CommonCompilationSettings {
77	  object VerilogPreprocessorDefine {
78	    def apply(name: String, value: String) = new VerilogPreprocessorDefine(name, Some(value))
79	    def apply(name: String) = new VerilogPreprocessorDefine(name, None)
80	  }
81	  case class VerilogPreprocessorDefine private (name: String, value: Option[String]) {
82	    final def toCommandlineArgument(backend: Backend): String = {
83	      value match {
84	        case Some(v) => s"+define+${backend.escapeDefine(name)}=${backend.escapeDefine(v)}"
85	        case None    => s"+define+${backend.escapeDefine(name)}"
86	      }
87	    }
88	  }
89	
90	  sealed trait OptimizationStyle
91	  object OptimizationStyle {
92	
93	    /** Use the default optimization level specified by the backend tool (i.e. Verilator or VCS) being used.
94	      */
95	    object Default extends OptimizationStyle
96	
97	    /** Optimize for compilation speed, which generally means disabling as many optimizations as possible.
98	      */
99	    object OptimizeForCompilationSpeed extends OptimizationStyle
100	
101	    /** Optimize for execution speed, which generally means enabling as many optimizations as possible.
102	      */
103	    object OptimizeForSimulationSpeed extends OptimizationStyle
104	  }
105	
106	  sealed trait AvailableParallelism
107	  object AvailableParallelism {
108	
109	    /** Use the default number of parallel processes specified by the backend tool (i.e. Verilator or VCS) being used.
110	      */
111	    object Default extends AvailableParallelism
112	
113	    /** Use up to specified number of parallel processes.
114	      */
115	    case class UpTo(value: Int) extends AvailableParallelism
116	  }
117	
118	  val default = CommonCompilationSettings()
119	
120	  /** Object that contains the implementation of SystemVerilog timescales.
121	    *
122	    * @see 1800-2023 Section 3.14
123	    */
124	  object Timescale {
125	    sealed trait Type
126	
127	    /** Represents legal magnitudes for Verilog times.
128	      *
129	      * These must be exactly 1, 10, or 100.
130	      */
131	    object Magnitude {
132	      sealed trait Type { this: Singleton =>
133	        def toInt: Int
134	        override def toString = toInt.toString
135	      }
136	      case object One extends Type {
137	        override def toInt = 1
138	      }
139	      case object Ten extends Type {
140	        override def toInt = 10
141	      }
142	      case object Hundred extends Type {
143	        override def toInt = 100
144	      }
145	    }
146	
147	    /** Represents legal units for Verilog times.
148	      *
149	      * These are allowed to be femtoseconds up to and including seconds in SI
150	      * units.
151	      */
152	    object Unit {
153	      sealed trait Type { this: Singleton =>
154	        override def toString = this.getClass.getSimpleName.dropRight(1)
155	
156	        def exponent: Int
157	      }
158	
159	      /** Seconds */
160	      case object s extends Type {
161	        override def exponent = 0
162	      }
163	
164	      /** Milliseconds, i.e., `10^-3` seconds */
165	      case object ms extends Type {
166	        override def exponent = -3
167	      }
168	
169	      /** Microseconds, i.e., `10^-6` seconds */
170	      case object us extends Type {
171	        override def exponent = -6
172	      }
173	
174	      /** Nanoseconds, i.e., `10^-9` seconds */
175	      case object ns extends Type {
176	        override def exponent = -9
177	      }
178	
179	      /** Picosedonds, i.e., `10^-12` seconds */
180	      case object ps extends Type {
181	        override def exponent = -12
182	      }
183	
184	      /** Femtoseconds, i.e., `10^-15` seconds */
185	      case object fs extends Type {
186	        override def exponent = -15
187	      }
188	    }
189	
190	    /** Represnts a Verilog notion of time.  This includes both a magnitude (the
191	      * time value) as well as the units.
192	      */
193	    case class Time(magnitude: Magnitude.Type, unit: Unit.Type) {
194	      override def toString = s"$magnitude$unit"
195	
196	      /** Return the time in femtoseconds.
197	        *
198	        * Femtoseconds are the unit because this is the smallest unit of time
199	        * that Verilog represents.  I.e., this is sufficient to represent any
200	        * time that Verilator understands without resorting to imprecise floats.
201	        */
202	      final def fs: BigInt = magnitude.toInt * BigInt(10).pow(unit.exponent - Unit.fs.exponent)
203	    }
204	
205	    /** A sane default timescale that allows for representing common, rounded clock
206	      * frequencies.
207	      */
208	    def default: Timescale =
209	      Timescale(unit = Time(Magnitude.One, Unit.ns), precision = Time(Magnitude.Hundred, Unit.ps))
210	  }
211	
212	  /** A SystemVerilog timescale.
213	    *
214	    * This is something that can be legally used as the argument of a ``
215	    * `timescale `` directive.
216	    *
217	    * @see 1800-2023 Section 3.14
218	    */
219	  case class Timescale(unit: Timescale.Time, precision: Timescale.Time) {
220	    require(unit.fs >= precision.fs, "the timescale unit must be greater than or equal to the timescale precision")
221	
222	    override def toString = s"$unit/$precision"
223	
224	    /** Return a decimal represntation of one timescale _precision_ in the units of
225	      * one timescale _unit_.
226	      */
227	    final def tickPrecision: Double = precision.fs.toDouble / unit.fs.toDouble
228	  }
229	
230	}
231	
232	/** A key/value pair representing a Verilog plusarg
233	  *
234	  * When `value` is a `Some`, this acts like a `$value$plusargs`.  When `value`
235	  * is `None`, this acts like a `$test$plusargs`.  I.e., when `None`, the
236	  * plusarg is simply set.  When `Some`, the plusarg has a value.
237	  *
238	  * @param name the name of the plusarg
239	  * @param value an optional value for the plusarg
240	  */
241	final class PlusArg(
242	  val name:  String,
243	  val value: Option[String] = None
244	) {
245	
246	  /** Return Verilator-compliant or VCS-compliant simulator options to set this
247	    * plusarg.
248	    */
249	  def simulatorFlags: String = (name +: value.toSeq).mkString("+", "=", "")
250	
251	}

253	trait Backend {
254	  type CompilationSettings <: Backend.Settings
255	  def generateParameters(
256	    outputBinaryName:        String,
257	    topModuleName:           String,
258	    additionalHeaderPaths:   Seq[String],
259	    commonSettings:          CommonCompilationSettings,
260	    backendSpecificSettings: CompilationSettings
261	  ): Backend.Parameters

263	  /** This function will be applied to all defines (both the keys and the values).
264	    * This can be used to workaround subtleties in how different simulators
265	    * parse defines and require different escaping.
266	    */
267	  def escapeDefine(string: String): String
268	
269	  /** A regular expression that indicates lines in a log file which indicate
270	    * assertion failure.
271	    */
272	  def assertionFailed: Regex
273	
274	}
275	
276	/** Type class to modify backend comopilation settings */
277	trait BackendSettingsModifications extends (Backend.Settings => Backend.Settings)
278	
279	object BackendSettingsModifications {
280	
281	  implicit def unmodified: BackendSettingsModifications = identity(_)
282	
283	}

285	final object Backend {
286	
287	  /** The super type of all backend-specific settings. */
288	  trait Settings extends Product
289	
290	  final case class Parameters(
291	    private[svsim] val compilerPath:         String,
292	    private[svsim] val compilerInvocation:   Parameters.Invocation,
293	    private[svsim] val simulationInvocation: Parameters.Invocation
294	  )
295	
296	  final object Parameters {
297	
298	    /**
299	      * Parameters for the invocation of a command-line tool. The constituent properties are private to `svsim` and not meant for external consumption (we may change this representation in the future, for example to [add convenient tracing functionality to make-replay](https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/issues/3150)).
300	      */
301	    final case class Invocation(
302	      private[svsim] val arguments:   Seq[String],
303	      private[svsim] val environment: Seq[(String, String)]
304	    )
305	  }
306	
307	  /**
308	    * A namespace for flags affecting which code in the harness is compiled.
309	    */
310	  object HarnessCompilationFlags {
311	
312	    /** Verilator support requires that we manually implement some SystemVerilog functions, such as `run_simulation` and `simulation_main`. These flags control the Verilator-specific code paths.
313	      */
314	    val enableVerilatorSupport = "SVSIM_ENABLE_VERILATOR_SUPPORT"
315	    val enableVerilatorTrace = "SVSIM_VERILATOR_TRACE_ENABLED"
316	
317	    /** This flag controls whether Verilator coverage data writing support is compiled into the simulation harness.
318	      */
319	    val enableVerilatorCoverage = "SVSIM_ENABLE_VERILATOR_COVERAGE_SUPPORT"
320	
321	    /** This flag controls if VCS-specifc code is compiled.
322	      */
323	    val enableVCSSupport = "SVSIM_ENABLE_VCS_SUPPORT"
324	
325	    /** Flags enabling various tracing mechanisms.
326	      * Note: These flags do not cause tracing to occur, they simply support for these tracing mechanisms in the harness.
327	      */
328	    val enableVcdTracingSupport = "SVSIM_ENABLE_VCD_TRACING_SUPPORT"
329	    val enableFstTracingSupport = "SVSIM_ENABLE_FST_TRACING_SUPPORT"
330	    val enableVpdTracingSupport = "SVSIM_ENABLE_VPD_TRACING_SUPPORT"
331	    val enableFsdbTracingSupport = "SVSIM_ENABLE_FSDB_TRACING_SUPPORT"
332	
333	    /** Verilator does not currently support delay (`#delay`) in DPI functions, so we omit the SystemVerilog definition of the `run_simulation` function and instead provide a C implementation.
334	      */
335	    val supportsDelayInPublicFunctions = "SVSIM_BACKEND_SUPPORTS_DELAY_IN_PUBLIC_FUNCTIONS"
336	  }
337	
338	  object Exceptions {
339	
340	    /** Indicates that a backend failed to initialize. */
341	    final class FailedInitialization private[svsim] (message: String) extends RuntimeException(message)
342	
343	  }
344	}

3	import java.io.{BufferedReader, BufferedWriter, File, FileWriter, InputStreamReader, PrintWriter}
