5	import java.nio.file.{FileVisitResult, FileVisitor, Files, Path, Paths, SimpleFileVisitor}
6	import java.lang.ProcessBuilder.Redirect
7	import java.util.Comparator
8	import scala.annotation.meta.param
9	import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._
10	import scala.sys.SystemProperties
11	
12	case class ModuleInfo(name: String, ports: Seq[ModuleInfo.Port]) {
13	  private[svsim] val instanceName = "dut"
14	}
15	object ModuleInfo {
16	  case class Port(name: String, isSettable: Boolean = false, isGettable: Boolean = false) {
17	    assert(name.matches("^[a-zA-Z0-9\\-_]*$"))
18	  }
19	}
20	
21	object Workspace {
22	  val testbenchModuleName: String = "svsimTestbench"
23	
24	  private class FileDeleter extends SimpleFileVisitor[Path] {
25	    override def visitFile(file: Path, attrs: BasicFileAttributes): FileVisitResult = {
26	      Files.delete(file)
27	      FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
28	    }
29	    override def postVisitDirectory(dir: Path, ioe: java.io.IOException): FileVisitResult = {
30	      try {
31	        Files.delete(dir)
32	      } catch {
33	        // This is best effort, leave directories if we can't delete them due to things like FUSE.
34	        case _: java.nio.file.DirectoryNotEmptyException => ()
35	      }
36	      FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
37	    }
38	  }
39	
40	  /**
41	    * Detect the project root (workspace) directory. It uses the `chisel.project.root` Java system property if it is set.
42	    * Otherwise, checks the environment variables `CHISEL_PROJECT_ROOT` (which can be set by user) and `MILL_WORKSPACE_ROOT` (set by mill).
43	    * If none of those are available, returns the current working directory.
44	    *
45	    * @return the absolute path to the project root or the current working directory
46	    */
47	  def getProjectRootOrCwd: Path = sys.props
48	    .get("chisel.project.root")
49	    .orElse(sys.env.get("CHISEL_PROJECT_ROOT"))
50	    .orElse(sys.env.get("MILL_WORKSPACE_ROOT"))
51	    .map(Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath.resolve(_))
52	    .getOrElse(Paths.get(""))
53	    .toAbsolutePath
54	}
55	final class Workspace(
56	  path: String,
57	  /** The prefix for the working directory used when invoking `compile`
58	    */
59	  val workingDirectoryPrefix: String = "workdir"
60	) {
61	
62	  val absolutePath =
63	    if (Paths.get(path).isAbsolute())
64	      path
65	    else
66	      s"${System.getProperty("user.dir")}/$path"
67	
68	  /** A directory where the user can store additional artifacts which are relevant to the primary sources (for instance, artifacts related to the generation of primary sources). These artifacts have no impact on the simulation, but it may be useful to group them with the other files generated by svsim for debugging purposes.
69	    */
70	  val supportArtifactsPath = s"$absolutePath/support-artifacts"
71	
72	  /** The directory containing user-provided source files used to compile the simulation when `compile` is called.
73	    */
74	  val primarySourcesPath = s"$absolutePath/primary-sources"
75	
76	  /** The directory containing code generated when calling `generateAdditionalSources`
77	    */
78	  val generatedSourcesPath = s"$absolutePath/generated-sources"
79	
80	  private var _moduleInfo: Option[ModuleInfo] = None
81	
82	  def reset() = {
83	    _moduleInfo = None
84	
85	    // Create a path type object from the absolute path string.
86	    val absolutePathObject = Paths.get(absolutePath)
87	    if (Files.exists(absolutePathObject)) {
88	      Files.walkFileTree(absolutePathObject, new Workspace.FileDeleter)
89	    }
90	
91	    val pathsToCreate = Seq(
92	      supportArtifactsPath,
93	      primarySourcesPath,
94	      generatedSourcesPath
95	    )
96	    pathsToCreate.map(new File(_)).foreach(_.mkdirs())
97	  }
98	
99	  private def copyResource(klass: Class[_], name: String, targetDirectory: String) = {
100	    val inputStream = klass.getResourceAsStream(name)
101	    if (inputStream == null) throw new java.io.FileNotFoundException(name)
102	    val file = new java.io.File(targetDirectory, name.split("/").last)
103	    val outputStream = new java.io.FileOutputStream(file)
104	    Iterator
105	      .continually(inputStream.read)
106	      .takeWhile(_ != -1)
107	      .foreach(outputStream.write)
108	    inputStream.close()
109	    outputStream.close()
110	  }
111	
112	  /** A helper method which copies the specified resource into the primary sources directory.
113	    */
114	  def addPrimarySourceFromResource(klass: Class[_], name: String) = {
115	    copyResource(klass, name, primarySourcesPath)
116	  }
117	
118	  /** `svsim` elaboration simply stores the provided `ModuleInfo` for use by the `compile` method. The idea is that packages that actually do elaboration (like Chisel) will add an overload of this method in an implicit class that then calls this method with the appropriate `ModuleInfo`.
119	    */
120	  def elaborate(moduleInfo: ModuleInfo) = {
121	    assert(_moduleInfo.isEmpty)
122	    _moduleInfo = Some(moduleInfo)
123	  }
124	
125	  /** Generate additional sources necessary for simulating the module.
126	    */
127	  //format: off
128	  final def generateAdditionalSources(timescale: Option[CommonCompilationSettings.Timescale]) = {
129	    val dut = _moduleInfo.get
130	    val ports = dut.ports.zipWithIndex
131	
132	    val systemVerilogTestbenchWriter = new LineWriter(s"$generatedSourcesPath/testbench.sv")
133	    try {
134	      val l = systemVerilogTestbenchWriter
135	
136	      l("module ", Workspace.testbenchModuleName, ";")
137	      for ((port, index) <- ports) {
138	        if (port.isSettable) {
139	      l("  reg  [$bits(", dut.instanceName, ".",  port.name, ")-1:0] ", port.name, " = '0;")
140	        } else {
141	      l("  wire [$bits(", dut.instanceName, ".",  port.name, ")-1:0] ", port.name, ";")
142	        }
143	      }
144	      l()
145	      l(dut.name, " ", dut.instanceName, " (")
146	      for ((port, index) <- ports) {
147	      l("    .", port.name, "(", port.name, ")", if (index != ports.length - 1) "," else "")
148	      }
149	      l(");")
150	      l()
151	      l("  import \"DPI-C\" context function void initTestBenchScope();")
152	      l("  initial")
153	      l("    initTestBenchScope();")
154	      for ((port, index) <- ports) {
155	      l("  // Port ", index.toHexString, ": ", port.name)
156	      l("  export \"DPI-C\" function getBitWidthImpl_", port.name, ";")
157	      l("  function void getBitWidthImpl_", port.name, ";")
158	      l("    output int value;")
159	      l("    value", " = $bits(", dut.instanceName, ".",  port.name, ");")
160	      l("  endfunction")
161	        if (port.isSettable) {
162	      l("  export \"DPI-C\" function setBitsImpl_", port.name, ";")
163	      l("  function void setBitsImpl_", port.name, ";")
164	      l("    input bit [$bits(", dut.instanceName, ".",  port.name, ")-1:0] value_", port.name, ";")
165	      l("    ", port.name, " = value_", port.name, ";")
166	      l("  endfunction")
167	        }
168	        if (port.isGettable) {
169	      l("  export \"DPI-C\" function getBitsImpl_", port.name, ";")
170	      l("  function void getBitsImpl_", port.name, ";")
171	      l("    output bit [$bits(", dut.instanceName, ".",  port.name, ")-1:0] value_", port.name, ";")
172	      l("    value_", port.name, " = ", port.name, ";")
173	      l("  endfunction")
174	        }
175	      l()
176	      }
177	      l("  // Simulation")
178	      l("  import \"DPI-C\" context task simulation_body();")
179	      l("  enum {INIT, RUN, DONE} simulationState = INIT;")
180	      l("  initial")
181	      l("    simulationState = RUN;")
182	      l("  always @(simulationState) begin")
183	      l("    if (simulationState == RUN) begin")
184	      l("      simulation_body();")
185	      l("      simulationState = DONE;")
186	      l("    end")
187	      l("  end")
188	      l("  import \"DPI-C\" context task simulation_final();")
189	      l("  final")
190	      l("    simulation_final();")
191	      l("  `ifdef ", Backend.HarnessCompilationFlags.supportsDelayInPublicFunctions)
192	      l("  export \"DPI-C\" task run_simulation;")
193	      l("  task run_simulation;")
194	      l("    input int timesteps;")
195	      l("    output int finish;")
196	      l(s"    #(timesteps*${timescale.map(_.tickPrecision).getOrElse(1)});")
197	      l("    finish = 0;")
198	      l("  endtask")
199	      l("  `else")
200	      l("  import \"DPI-C\" function void run_simulation(input int timesteps, output int done);")
201	      l("  `endif")
202	      l()
203	
204	      l("  // Tracing")
205	      l("  int traceSupported = 0;")
206	      l("  export \"DPI-C\" function simulation_initializeTrace;")
207	      l("  function void simulation_initializeTrace;")
208	      l("    input string traceFilePath;")
209	      l("    `ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_FST_TRACING_SUPPORT")
210	      l("      $dumpfile({traceFilePath,\".fst\"});")
211	      l("      $dumpvars(0, ", dut.instanceName,");")
212	      l("      traceSupported = 1;")
213	      l("    `elsif SVSIM_ENABLE_VCD_TRACING_SUPPORT")
214	      l("      $dumpfile({traceFilePath,\".vcd\"});")
215	      l("      $dumpvars(0, ", dut.instanceName,");")
216	      l("      traceSupported = 1;")
217	      l("    `endif")
218	      l("    `ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_VPD_TRACING_SUPPORT")
219	      l("      $vcdplusfile({traceFilePath,\".vpd\"});")
220	      l("      $dumpvars(0, ", dut.instanceName,");")
221	      l("      $vcdpluson(0, ", dut.instanceName,");")
222	      l("      traceSupported = 1;")
223	      l("    `endif")
224	      l("    `ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_FSDB_TRACING_SUPPORT")
225	      l("      $fsdbDumpfile({traceFilePath,\".fsdb\"});")
226	      l("      $fsdbDumpvars(0, ", dut.instanceName,", \"+all\");")
227	      l("      traceSupported = 1;")
228	      l("    `endif")
229	      l("  endfunction")
230	      l("  export \"DPI-C\" function simulation_enableTrace;")
231	      l("  function void simulation_enableTrace;")
232	      l("    output int success;")
233	      l("    success = traceSupported;")
234	      l("    `ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_VCD_TRACING_SUPPORT")
235	      l("    $dumpon;")
236	      l("    `elsif SVSIM_ENABLE_FST_TRACING_SUPPORT")
237	      l("    $dumpon;")
238	      l("    `elsif SVSIM_ENABLE_VPD_TRACING_SUPPORT")
239	      l("    $dumpon;")
240	      l("    `endif")
241	      l("    `ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_FSDB_TRACING_SUPPORT")
242	      l("    $fsdbDumpon;")
243	      l("    `endif")
244	      l("  endfunction")
245	      l("  export \"DPI-C\" function simulation_disableTrace;")
246	      l("  function void simulation_disableTrace;")
247	      l("    output int success;")
248	      l("    success = traceSupported;")
249	      l("    `ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_VCD_TRACING_SUPPORT")
250	      l("    $dumpoff;")
251	      l("    `elsif SVSIM_ENABLE_FST_TRACING_SUPPORT")
252	      l("    $dumpoff;")
253	      l("    `elsif SVSIM_ENABLE_VPD_TRACING_SUPPORT")
254	      l("    $dumpoff;")
255	      l("    `endif")
256	      l("    `ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_FSDB_TRACING_SUPPORT")
257	      l("    $fsdbDumpoff;")
258	      l("    `endif")
259	      l("  endfunction")
260	      l()
261	      l("endmodule")
262	    } finally {
263	      // `BufferedWriter` closes the underlying `FileWriter` when closed.
264	      systemVerilogTestbenchWriter.close()
265	    }
266	
267	    // This object creates a wrapper function for exported DPI functions to
268	    // properly set the scope to testbench top before calling DPI functions.
269	    object CreateFunctionForPort {
270	      def createGetBits(portName: String): String = {
271	        f"""void getBits_$portName(svBitVecVal* result) {
272	           svScope prev = setScopeToTestBench();
273	           getBitsImpl_$portName(result);
274	           svSetScope(prev);
275	        }"""
276	      }
277	
278	      def createGetBitWidth(portName: String): String = {
279	        f"""void getBitWidth_$portName(int* result) {
280	           svScope prev = setScopeToTestBench();
281	           getBitWidthImpl_$portName(result);
282	           svSetScope(prev);
283	        }"""
284	      }
285	      def createSetBits(portName: String): String = {
286	        f"""void setBits_$portName(const svBitVecVal* data) {
287	           svScope prev = setScopeToTestBench();
288	           setBitsImpl_$portName(data);
289	           svSetScope(prev);
290	        }"""
291	      }
292	    }
293	
294	    val cDPIBridgeWriter = new LineWriter(s"$generatedSourcesPath/c-dpi-bridge.cpp")
295	    try {
296	      val l = cDPIBridgeWriter
297	      l("#include <stdint.h>")
298	      l()
299	      l("#ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_VERILATOR_SUPPORT")
300	      l("#include \"verilated-sources/VsvsimTestbench__Dpi.h\"")
301	      l("#endif")
302	      l("#ifdef SVSIM_ENABLE_VCS_SUPPORT")
303	      l("#include \"vc_hdrs.h\"")
304	      l("#endif")
305	      l()
306	      l("extern \"C\" {")
307	      l(" svScope setScopeToTestBench();")
308	      for ((port, index) <- ports) {
309	      l(CreateFunctionForPort.createGetBitWidth(port.name))
310	        if (port.isGettable) {
311	      l(CreateFunctionForPort.createGetBits(port.name))
312	        }
313	        if (port.isSettable) {
314	      l(CreateFunctionForPort.createSetBits(port.name))
315	        }
316	      }
317	      l()
318	      l("int port_getter(int id, int *bitWidth, void (**getter)(uint8_t*)) {")
319	      l("  switch (id) {")
320	      for ((port, index) <- ports.filter(_._1.isGettable)) {
321	      l("    case ", index.toString(), ": // ", port.name)
322	      l("      getBitWidth_", port.name, "(bitWidth);")
323	      l("      *getter = (void(*)(uint8_t*))getBits_", port.name, ";")
324	      l("      return 0;")
325	      }
326	      l("    default:")
327	      l("      return -1;")
328	      l("  }")
329	      l("}")
330	      l()
331	      l("int port_setter(int id, int *bitWidth, void (**setter)(const uint8_t*)) {")
332	      l("  switch (id) {")
333	      for ((port, index) <- ports.filter(_._1.isSettable)) {
334	      l("    case ", index.toString(), ": // ", port.name)
335	      l("      getBitWidth_", port.name, "(bitWidth);")
336	      l("      *setter = (void(*)(const uint8_t*))setBits_", port.name, ";")
337	      l("      return 0;")
338	      }
339	      l("    default:")
340	      l("      return -1;")
341	      l("  }")
342	      l("}")
343	      l()
344	      l("} // extern \"C\"")
345	      l()
346	
347	    } finally {
348	      cDPIBridgeWriter.close()
349	    }
350	
351	    copyResource(this.getClass, "/simulation-driver.cpp", generatedSourcesPath)
352	  }
353	  //format: on
354	
355	  /** Shallow copy the sources from this workspace to a new one. Primary sources are symlinked to the
356	   *  new directory; nothing else is copied.
357	   */
358	  def shallowCopy(newPath: String, workingDirectoryPrefix: String = this.workingDirectoryPrefix): Workspace = {
359	    val newWorkspace = new Workspace(newPath, workingDirectoryPrefix)
360	    newWorkspace.reset()
361	
362	    val newPrimarySources = new File(newWorkspace.primarySourcesPath)
363	
364	    val sourcePrimarySources = new File(this.primarySourcesPath)
365	    if (sourcePrimarySources.exists()) {
366	      Files
367	        .walk(sourcePrimarySources.toPath)
368	        .filter(Files.isRegularFile(_))
369	        .forEach { target =>
370	          val relativePath = sourcePrimarySources.toPath.relativize(target)
371	          val sourcePath = Paths.get(newPrimarySources.getPath, relativePath.toString)
372	          sourcePath.getParent.toFile.mkdirs()
373	          val relativeTarget = sourcePath.getParent.relativize(target)
374	          Files.createSymbolicLink(sourcePath, relativeTarget)
375	        }
376	    }
377	
378	    newWorkspace
379	  }
380	
381	  /** Compiles the simulation using the specified backend.
382	    *
383	    * @param outputTag A string which will be used to tag the output directory. This enables compiling and simulating the same workspace with multiple backends.
384	    */
385	  def compile[T <: Backend](
386	    backend: T
387	  )(
388	    workingDirectoryTag:              String,
389	    commonSettings:                   CommonCompilationSettings,
390	    backendSpecificSettings:          backend.CompilationSettings,
391	    customSimulationWorkingDirectory: Option[String],
392	    verbose:                          Boolean
393	  ): Simulation = {
394	    val moduleInfo = _moduleInfo.get
395	    val workingDirectoryPath = s"$absolutePath/$workingDirectoryPrefix-$workingDirectoryTag"
396	    val workingDirectory = new File(workingDirectoryPath)
397	    workingDirectory.mkdir()
398	
399	    val parameters = backend.generateParameters(
400	      outputBinaryName = "simulation",
401	      topModuleName = Workspace.testbenchModuleName,
402	      additionalHeaderPaths = Seq(workingDirectoryPath),
403	      commonSettings = commonSettings,
404	      backendSpecificSettings = backendSpecificSettings
405	    )
406	
407	    // Find all the source files by walking the build directory.  The behavior
408	    // of which directories are visited and which files are included is
409	    // controlled by fields in `CommonCompilationSettings`.
410	    val sourceFiles = scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer.empty[String]
411	    val fileFilter: PartialFunction[File, Boolean] = commonSettings.fileFilter.orElse { case _ => true }
412	    val directoryFilter: PartialFunction[File, Boolean] = commonSettings.directoryFilter.orElse { case _: File =>
413	      true
414	    }
415	    class DirectoryVisitor extends FileVisitor[Path] {
416	
417	      override def visitFile(file: Path, attrs: BasicFileAttributes): FileVisitResult = {
418	        if (fileFilter(file.toFile))
419	          sourceFiles += workingDirectory.toPath().relativize(file).toString()
420	        FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
421	      }
422	
423	      override def preVisitDirectory(dir: Path, attrs: BasicFileAttributes): FileVisitResult = {
424	        if (directoryFilter(dir.toFile))
425	          FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
426	        else
427	          FileVisitResult.SKIP_SUBTREE
428	      }
429	
430	      override def postVisitDirectory(dir: Path, ioe: java.io.IOException): FileVisitResult = {
431	        FileVisitResult.CONTINUE
432	      }
433	
434	      override def visitFileFailed(file: Path, ioe: java.io.IOException): FileVisitResult = {
435	        throw ioe
436	      }
437	
438	    }
439	
440	    for (dir <- Seq(primarySourcesPath, generatedSourcesPath)) {
441	      Files.walkFileTree(Paths.get(dir), new DirectoryVisitor)
442	    }
443	
444	    val traceFileStem = commonSettings.simulationSettings.traceFileStem
445	    val simulationEnvironment = Seq(
446	      "SVSIM_SIMULATION_LOG" -> s"$workingDirectoryPath/simulation-log.txt",
447	      // The simulation driver appends the appropriate extension to the file path
448	      "SVSIM_SIMULATION_TRACE" -> traceFileStem
449	    ) ++ parameters.simulationInvocation.environment
450	
451	    val sourceFilesFilelistWriter = new LineWriter(s"$workingDirectoryPath/sourceFiles.F")
452	    try {
453	      val l = sourceFilesFilelistWriter
454	      sourceFiles.foreach(l(_))
455	      l()
456	    } finally {
457	      sourceFilesFilelistWriter.close()
458	    }
459	
460	    // Emit Makefile for debugging (will be emitted even if compile fails)
461	    val makefileWriter = new LineWriter(s"$workingDirectoryPath/Makefile")
462	    try {
463	      val l = makefileWriter
464	      l("# This Makefile enables lightweight debugging of `svsim` tests. ")
465	      l("# To rebuild the simulation run `make simulation` in this directory.")
466	      l("# To replay the simulation run `make replay` in this directory.")
467	      l(
468	        "# Changes to `generated-sources` and `primary-sources` will be picked up when running `make replay` and `make simulation`. This is useful for debugging issues. You can also freely add, remove or change any of the arguments to the backend or simulation in the targets below."
469	      )
470	      l()
471	      l(".PHONY: clean simulation replay")
472	      l()
473	      //format: off
474	      // For this debug flow, we rebuild the simulation from scratch every time, to avoid issues if the simulation was originally compiled in a different environment, like using SiFive's `wake`.
475	      l("clean:")
476	      // Add check if OS is windows, since the command syntax is different
477	      if (System.getProperty("os.name").toLowerCase.contains("win")) {
478	        l("\tfor /f \"delims=\" %i in ('dir /b /a-d ^| findstr /v Makefile ^| findstr /v execution-script.txt') do del \"%i\"")
479	        l("\tfor /d %i in (*) do rmdir /s /q \"%i\"")
480	      } else {
481	        l("\tls . | grep -v Makefile | grep -v execution-script.txt | grep -v sourceFiles.F | xargs rm -rf")
482	      }
483	      l()
484	      l("simulation: clean")
485	      l("\t$(compilerEnvironment) \\")
486	      l("\t", parameters.compilerPath, " \\")
487	      for (argument <- parameters.compilerInvocation.arguments) {
488	        val sanitizedArugment = argument
489	          .replace("$", "$$")
490	          .replace("'", "'\\''")
491	          .replace(workingDirectoryPath, "$(shell pwd)")
492	        l("\t\t'", sanitizedArugment, "' \\")
493	      }
494	      l("'-F' \\")
495	      l(s"\t\t'${sourceFilesFilelistWriter.path}'")
496	      l()
497	      l("replay: simulation")
498	      val executionScriptPath = "$(shell pwd)/execution-script.txt"
499	      customSimulationWorkingDirectory match {
500	        case None =>
501	        case Some(value) => {
502	          // Calculate relative path, to avoid being broken by wake's directory shenanigans
503	          val relativePath = workingDirectory.toPath().relativize(Paths.get(value)).toString()
504	          l("\tcd ", relativePath, " && \\")
505	        }
506	      }
507	      l("\tcat ", executionScriptPath, " | { grep '^#' || true; } && \\")
508	      l("\tcat ", executionScriptPath, " | sed -n 's/^[0-9]*> \\(.*\\)/\\1/p' | \\")
509	      l("\t\t$(simulationEnvironment) $(shell pwd)/simulation \\")
510	      for (argument <- parameters.simulationInvocation.arguments) {
511	      l("\t\t\t'", argument.replace("$", "$$"), "' \\")
512	      }
513	      l()
514	      l("compilerEnvironment = \\")
515	      for (((name, value), index) <- parameters.compilerInvocation.environment.zipWithIndex) {
516	      l("\t", name, "=", value, if (index != parameters.compilerInvocation.environment.length - 1) " \\" else "")
517	      }
518	      l()
519	      l("simulationEnvironment = \\")
520	      for (((name, value), index) <- simulationEnvironment.zipWithIndex) {
521	        val sanitizedValue = value.replace(workingDirectoryPath, "$(shell pwd)")
522	        l("\t", name, "=", sanitizedValue, if (index != simulationEnvironment.length - 1) " \\" else "")
523	      }
524	      l()
525	      //format: on
526	    } finally {
527	      makefileWriter.close()
528	    }
529	
530	    /**
531	      * Use the generated Makefile to compile the simulation, since this exercises the Makefile codepath and makes it less likely that we will break `make replay`.
532	      */
533	    val processBuilder = new ProcessBuilder("make", "-C", workingDirectoryPath, "simulation")
534	    processBuilder.redirectErrorStream(true)
535	    val process = processBuilder.start()
536	    @scala.annotation.nowarn(
537	      "msg=Use `scala.jdk.CollectionConverters` instead"
538	    )
539	    def readLogLines() = {
540	      val sourceLocationRegex = "[\\./]*generated-sources/".r
541	      new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))
542	        .lines()
543	        .map(sourceLocationRegex.replaceFirstIn(_, ""))
544	        .map { line =>
545	          if (verbose) {
546	            println(line)
547	          }
548	          line
549	        }
550	        .iterator()
551	        .asScala
552	        .toSeq
553	    }
554	    val compilationLogLines = readLogLines()
555	    process.waitFor()
556	    val compilationLogWriter = new PrintWriter(
557	      new BufferedWriter(
558	        new FileWriter(new File(s"$workingDirectoryPath/compilation-log.txt"))
559	      )
560	    )
561	    compilationLogLines.foreach(compilationLogWriter.println)
562	    compilationLogWriter.close()
563	    if (process.exitValue() != 0) {
564	      throw new Exception(compilationLogLines.mkString("\n"))
565	    }
566	
567	    new Simulation(
568	      executableName = "simulation",
569	      settings = Simulation.Settings(
570	        customWorkingDirectory = customSimulationWorkingDirectory,
571	        arguments = parameters.simulationInvocation.arguments,
572	        environment = simulationEnvironment.toMap
573	      ),
574	      workingDirectoryPath = workingDirectoryPath,
575	      moduleInfo = moduleInfo
576	    )
577	  }
578	
579	}
580	
581	/** A micro-DSL for writing files.
582	  */
583	private class LineWriter(val path: String) {
584	  private val wrapped = new BufferedWriter(new FileWriter(path, false))
585	  def apply(components: String*) = {
586	    components.foreach(wrapped.write)
587	    wrapped.newLine()
588	  }
589	  def close() = wrapped.close()
590	}
591

content.text: 现在我来阅读用户使用的 API 文件：`PeekPokeAPI`、`SimulatorAPI`、`ControlAPI` 以及 `scalatest` 包，以便全面了解情况。

content.content:
1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	
3	package chisel3.simulator
4	
5	import chisel3.{Module, RawModule, SimulationTestHarnessInterface}
6	import chisel3.experimental.inlinetest.{HasTests, SimulatedTest, TestChoice}
7	import chisel3.simulator.stimulus.{ResetProcedure, SimulationTestStimulus}
8	import chisel3.testing.HasTestingDirectory
9	import chisel3.util.simpleClassName
10	import java.nio.file.Files
11	
12	trait SimulatorAPI {
13	
14	  /** Simulate a [[RawModule]] without any initialization procedure.
15	    *
16	    * Use of this method is not advised when [[simulate]] can be used instead.
17	    * This method may cause problems with certain simulators as it is up to the
18	    * user to ensure that race conditions will not exist during the time zero
19	    * and reset procedure.
20	    *
21	    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
22	    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
23	    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
24	    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
25	    * @param subdirectory an optional subdirectory for the test.  This will be a
26	    * subdirectory under what is provided by `testingDirectory`.
27	    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
28	    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to
29	    * change the behavior of where files will be created
30	    *
31	    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
32	    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
33	    */
34	  def simulateRaw[T <: RawModule](
35	    module:       => T,
36	    chiselOpts:   Array[String] = Array.empty,
37	    firtoolOpts:  Array[String] = Array.empty,
38	    settings:     Settings[T] = Settings.defaultRaw[T],
39	    subdirectory: Option[String] = None
40	  )(stimulus: (T) => Unit)(
41	    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
42	    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
43	    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
44	    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,

47	  ): Unit = {

51	      case None         => testingDirectory
52	    }
53	
54	    hasSimulator
55	      .getSimulator(modifiedTestingDirectory)
56	      .simulate(module = module, chiselOpts = chiselOpts, firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts, settings = settings) { module =>
57	        stimulus(module.wrapped)
58	      }
59	      .result
60	  }
61	
62	  /** Simulate a [[Module]] using a standard initialization procedure.
63	    *
64	    * For details of the initialization procedure see [[ResetProcedure]].
65	    *
66	    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
67	    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel

69	    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
70	    * @param additionalResetCycles a number of _additional_ cycles to assert

73	    * subdirectory under what is provided by `testingDirectory`.
74	    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
75	    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to
76	    * change the behavior of where files will be created
77	    *
78	    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
79	    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
80	    */
81	  def simulate[T <: Module](
82	    module:                => T,
83	    chiselOpts:            Array[String] = Array.empty,
84	    firtoolOpts:           Array[String] = Array.empty,
85	    settings:              Settings[T] = Settings.default[T],
86	    additionalResetCycles: Int = 0,
87	    subdirectory:          Option[String] = None
88	  )(stimulus: (T) => Unit)(
89	    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
90	    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
91	    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
92	    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,
93	    commonSettingsModifications:  svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
94	    backendSettingsModifications: svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
95	  ): Unit = simulateRaw(
96	    module = module,
97	    chiselOpts = chiselOpts,
98	    firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts,
99	    settings = settings,
100	    subdirectory = subdirectory
101	  ) { dut =>
102	    ResetProcedure.module(additionalResetCycles)(dut)
103	    stimulus(dut)
104	  }
105	
106	  /** Simulate a test using standard simulation testharness stimulus.
107	    *
108	    * For details of the initialization procedure see [[ResetProcedure]].
109	    *
110	    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
111	    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
112	    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
113	    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
114	    * @param additionalResetCycles a number of _additional_ cycles to assert
115	    * reset for

117	    * subdirectory under what is provided by `testingDirectory`.
118	    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
119	    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to
120	    * change the behavior of where files will be created
121	    *
122	    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
123	    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
124	    */
125	  def simulateTest[T <: RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface](
126	    module:                => T,
127	    timeout:               Int,
128	    chiselOpts:            Array[String] = Array.empty,
129	    firtoolOpts:           Array[String] = Array.empty,
130	    settings:              Settings[T] = Settings.defaultTest[T],
131	    additionalResetCycles: Int = 0,
132	    subdirectory:          Option[String] = None
133	  )(
134	    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
135	    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
136	    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
137	    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,
138	    commonSettingsModifications:  svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
139	    backendSettingsModifications: svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
140	  ): Unit = simulateRaw(

142	    chiselOpts = chiselOpts,
143	    firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts,
144	    settings = settings,
145	    subdirectory = subdirectory
146	  ) { dut =>
147	    ResetProcedure.testHarness(additionalResetCycles)(dut)
148	    SimulationTestStimulus(timeout)(dut)
149	  }

151	  /** Simulate the tests of a [[HasTests]] module.
152	    *
153	    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
154	    * @param test the choice of which test(s) to run
155	    * @param timeout number of cycles after which the test fails if unfinished
156	    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
157	    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
158	    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
159	    * @param additionalResetCycles a number of _additional_ cycles to assert

163	    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
164	    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to

166	    *
167	    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
168	    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
169	    */
170	  def simulateTests[T <: RawModule with HasTests](
171	    module:      => T,
172	    tests:       TestChoice.Type,
173	    timeout:     Int,
174	    chiselOpts:  Array[String] = Array.empty,
175	    firtoolOpts: Array[String] = Array.empty,
176	    settings: Settings[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface] =
177	      Settings.defaultRaw[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface],
178	    additionalResetCycles: Int = 0,
179	    subdirectory:          Option[String] = None
180	  )(
181	    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
182	    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
183	    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
184	    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,
185	    commonSettingsModifications:  svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
186	    backendSettingsModifications: svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
187	  ) = {
188	    val modifiedTestingDirectory = subdirectory match {
189	      case Some(subdir) => testingDirectory.withSubdirectory(subdir)
190	      case None         => testingDirectory
191	    }
192	
193	    hasSimulator
194	      .getSimulator(modifiedTestingDirectory)
195	      .simulateTests(
196	        module = module,
197	        includeTestGlobs = tests.globs,
198	        chiselOpts = chiselOpts,
199	        firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts,
200	        settings = settings
201	      ) { dut =>
202	        ResetProcedure.testHarness(additionalResetCycles, period = 10)(dut.wrapped)
203	        SimulationTestStimulus.testHarness(timeout, period = 10)(dut.wrapped)
204	      }
205	  }

// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

package chisel3.simulator

import chisel3.{Module, RawModule, SimulationTestHarnessInterface}
import chisel3.experimental.inlinetest.{HasTests, SimulatedTest, TestChoice}
import chisel3.simulator.stimulus.{ResetProcedure, SimulationTestStimulus}
import chisel3.testing.HasTestingDirectory
import chisel3.util.simpleClassName
import java.nio.file.Files

trait SimulatorAPI {

  /** Simulate a [[RawModule]] without any initialization procedure.
    *
    * Use of this method is not advised when [[simulate]] can be used instead.
    * This method may cause problems with certain simulators as it is up to the
    * user to ensure that race conditions will not exist during the time zero
    * and reset procedure.
    *
    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
    * @param subdirectory an optional subdirectory for the test.  This will be a
    * subdirectory under what is provided by `testingDirectory`.
    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to
    * change the behavior of where files will be created
    *
    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
    */
  def simulateRaw[T <: RawModule](
    module:       => T,
    chiselOpts:   Array[String] = Array.empty,
    firtoolOpts:  Array[String] = Array.empty,
    settings:     Settings[T] = Settings.defaultRaw[T],
    subdirectory: Option[String] = None
  )(stimulus: (T) => Unit)(
    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,
    commonSettingsModifications:  svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
    backendSettingsModifications: svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
  ): Unit = {

    val modifiedTestingDirectory = subdirectory match {
      case Some(subdir) => testingDirectory.withSubdirectory(subdir)
      case None         => testingDirectory
    }

    hasSimulator
      .getSimulator(modifiedTestingDirectory)
      .simulate(module = module, chiselOpts = chiselOpts, firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts, settings = settings) { module =>
        stimulus(module.wrapped)
      }
      .result
  }

  /** Simulate a [[Module]] using a standard initialization procedure.
    *
    * For details of the initialization procedure see [[ResetProcedure]].
    *
    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
    * @param additionalResetCycles a number of _additional_ cycles to assert
    * reset for
    * @param subdirectory an optional subdirectory for the test.  This will be a
    * subdirectory under what is provided by `testingDirectory`.
    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to
    * change the behavior of where files will be created
    *
    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
    */
  def simulate[T <: Module](
    module:                => T,
    chiselOpts:            Array[String] = Array.empty,
    firtoolOpts:           Array[String] = Array.empty,
    settings:              Settings[T] = Settings.default[T],
    additionalResetCycles: Int = 0,
    subdirectory:          Option[String] = None
  )(stimulus: (T) => Unit)(
    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,
    commonSettingsModifications:  svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
    backendSettingsModifications: svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
  ): Unit = simulateRaw(
    module = module,
    chiselOpts = chiselOpts,
    firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts,
    settings = settings,
    subdirectory = subdirectory
  ) { dut =>
    ResetProcedure.module(additionalResetCycles)(dut)
    stimulus(dut)
  }

  /** Simulate a test using standard simulation testharness stimulus.
    *
    * For details of the initialization procedure see [[ResetProcedure]].
    *
    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
    * @param additionalResetCycles a number of _additional_ cycles to assert
    * reset for
    * @param subdirectory an optional subdirectory for the test.  This will be a
    * subdirectory under what is provided by `testingDirectory`.
    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to
    * change the behavior of where files will be created
    *
    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
    */
  def simulateTest[T <: RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface](
    module:                => T,
    timeout:               Int,
    chiselOpts:            Array[String] = Array.empty,
    firtoolOpts:           Array[String] = Array.empty,
    settings:              Settings[T] = Settings.defaultTest[T],
    additionalResetCycles: Int = 0,
    subdirectory:          Option[String] = None
  )(
    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,
    commonSettingsModifications:  svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
    backendSettingsModifications: svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
  ): Unit = simulateRaw(
    module = module,
    chiselOpts = chiselOpts,
    firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts,
    settings = settings,
    subdirectory = subdirectory
  ) { dut =>
    ResetProcedure.testHarness(additionalResetCycles)(dut)
    SimulationTestStimulus(timeout)(dut)
  }

  /** Simulate the tests of a [[HasTests]] module.
    *
    * @param module the Chisel module to generate
    * @param test the choice of which test(s) to run
    * @param timeout number of cycles after which the test fails if unfinished
    * @param chiselOpts command line options to pass to Chisel
    * @param firtoolOpts command line options to pass to firtool
    * @param settings ChiselSim-related settings used for simulation
    * @param additionalResetCycles a number of _additional_ cycles to assert
    * reset for
    * @param subdirectory an optional subdirectory for the test.  This will be a
    * subdirectory under what is provided by `testingDirectory`.
    * @param stimulus directed stimulus to use
    * @param testingDirectory a type class implementation that can be used to
    * change the behavior of where files will be created
    *
    * @note Take care when passing `chiselOpts`.  The following options are set
    * by default and if you set incompatible options, the simulation will fail.
    */
  def simulateTests[T <: RawModule with HasTests](
    module:      => T,
    tests:       TestChoice.Type,
    timeout:     Int,
    chiselOpts:  Array[String] = Array.empty,
    firtoolOpts: Array[String] = Array.empty,
    settings: Settings[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface] =
      Settings.defaultRaw[RawModule with SimulationTestHarnessInterface],
    additionalResetCycles: Int = 0,
    subdirectory:          Option[String] = None
  )(
    implicit hasSimulator:        HasSimulator,
    testingDirectory:             HasTestingDirectory,
    chiselOptsModifications:      ChiselOptionsModifications,
    firtoolOptsModifications:     FirtoolOptionsModifications,
    commonSettingsModifications:  svsim.CommonSettingsModifications,
    backendSettingsModifications: svsim.BackendSettingsModifications
  ) = {
    val modifiedTestingDirectory = subdirectory match {
      case Some(subdir) => testingDirectory.withSubdirectory(subdir)
      case None         => testingDirectory
    }

    hasSimulator
      .getSimulator(modifiedTestingDirectory)
      .simulateTests(
        module = module,
        includeTestGlobs = tests.globs,
        chiselOpts = chiselOpts,
        firtoolOpts = firtoolOpts,
        settings = settings
      ) { dut =>
        ResetProcedure.testHarness(additionalResetCycles, period = 10)(dut.wrapped)
        SimulationTestStimulus.testHarness(timeout, period = 10)(dut.wrapped)
      }
  }
}

1	package chisel3.simulator
2	
3	import chisel3._
4	import chisel3.experimental.BundleLiterals
5	import chisel3.experimental.VecLiterals._
6	import chisel3.experimental.{SourceInfo, SourceLine}
7	import chisel3.internal.ExceptionHelpers
8	import chisel3.internal.binding.DontCareBinding
9	import firrtl.options.StageUtils.dramaticMessage
10	import svsim._
11	
12	import scala.util.control.NoStackTrace
13	import scala.language.implicitConversions
14	
15	trait Peekable[T <: Data] {
16	
17	  /**
18	    * Get the value of a data port as a literal.
19	    *
20	    * @return the value of the peeked data
21	    */
22	  def peek()(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): T
23	
24	  /**
25	      * Expect the value of a data port to be equal to the expected value.
26	      *
27	      * @param expected the expected value
28	      * @throws FailedExpectationException if the observed value does not match the expected value
29	      */
30	  def expect(expected: T)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit = expect(expected, "")
31	
32	  /**
33	  * Expect the value of a data port to be equal to the expected value.
34	  *
35	  * @param expected the expected value
36	  * @param message a message for the failure case
37	  * @throws FailedExpectationException if the observed value does not match the expected value
38	  */
39	  def expect(expected: T, message: String)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit
40	
41	  /**
42	  * Expect the value of a data port to be equal to the expected value.
43	  *
44	  * @param expected the expected value
45	  * @param format formatting strategy for rendered values in failure output
46	  * @throws FailedExpectationException if the observed value does not match the expected value
47	  */
48	  def expect(expected: T, format: ExpectationValueFormat.Type)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
49	    expect(expected, "", format)
50	
51	  /**
52	  * Expect the value of a data port to be equal to the expected value.
53	  *
54	  * @param expected the expected value
55	  * @param message a message for the failure case
56	  * @param format formatting strategy for rendered values in failure output
57	  * @throws FailedExpectationException if the observed value does not match the expected value
58	  */
59	  def expect(expected: T, message: String, format: ExpectationValueFormat.Type)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
60	    expect(expected, message)
61	
62	  private[simulator] def dataToString(value: Data): String = {
63	    value match {
64	      case x: Bundle =>
65	        x.elements.map { case (name, elt) =>
66	          s"$name: ${dataToString(elt)}"
67	        }.mkString("{", ", ", "}")
68	      case x: Vec[_]   => x.getElements.map(dataToString).mkString("[", ", ", "]")
69	      case x: EnumType => x.toString
70	      case x if x.isLit => x.litValue.toString
71	      case _            => value.toString
72	    }
73	  }
74	}
75	
76	trait Pokable[T <: Data] {
77	
78	  /**
79	    * Sets the value of a data port.
80	    *
81	    * @param literal the value to set, which must be a literal
82	    */
83	  def poke(literal: T): Unit
84	}
85	
86	sealed trait AnyTestableData[T <: Data] {
87	  protected def data: T
88	
89	  protected def simulatedModule: AnySimulatedModule = AnySimulatedModule.current
90	
91	  protected def simulationPort: Simulation.Port = simulatedModule.port(data)
92	}
93	
94	trait PeekPokable[T <: Data] extends Peekable[T] with Pokable[T] with AnyTestableData[T]
95	
96	trait PeekPokeApiException extends NoStackTrace
97	
98	/** Formatting options for values rendered by `expect` failure messages.
99	  */
100	object ExpectationValueFormat {
101	
102	  /** Context passed to custom formatters.
103	    *
104	    * @param chiselType Chisel data type string (e.g. `UInt<32>`)
105	    * @param signedValue Numeric value as observed by the simulator for this data type
106	    * @param bitWidth Bit width of the rendered value
107	    * @param isSigned Whether the data type is signed
108	    * @param displayString Value rendered using the decimal display for this context
109	    */
110	  final case class Value(
111	    chiselType:    String,
112	    signedValue:   BigInt,
113	    bitWidth:      Int,
114	    isSigned:      Boolean,
115	    displayString: String
116	  ) {
117	
118	    /** Value interpreted as an unsigned integer with `bitWidth` bits. */
119	    val unsignedValue: BigInt = {
120	      if (bitWidth <= 0) {
121	        signedValue
122	      } else {
123	        val mask = (BigInt(1) << bitWidth) - 1
124	        signedValue & mask
125	      }
126	    }
127	  }
128	
129	  /** Raw failure context passed to formatters. */
130	  final case class Failure(observed: Value, expected: Value)
131	
132	  /** Fully rendered failure output. */
133	  final case class Rendered(observed: String, expected: String, message: String)
134	
135	  /** Value format type. */
136	  sealed trait Type {
137	    private[simulator] def render(failure: Failure): Rendered
138	  }
139	
140	  /** Render values using their decimal display. */
141	  object Dec extends Type {
142	    override private[simulator] def render(failure: Failure): Rendered =
143	      renderValues(failure)(_.displayString)
144	  }
145	
146	  /** Render the numeric part as hexadecimal (prefixed with `0x`) and grouped by bytes. */
147	  object Hex extends Type {
148	    override private[simulator] def render(failure: Failure): Rendered =
149	      renderValues(failure)(value => withType(value.chiselType, s"0x${formatGroupedHexDigits(value)}"))
150	  }
151	
152	  /** Render the numeric part as binary (prefixed with `0b`). */
153	  object Bin extends Type {
154	    override private[simulator] def render(failure: Failure): Rendered =
155	      renderValues(failure)(value => withType(value.chiselType, s"0b${formatGroupedBinaryDigits(value)}"))
156	  }
157	
158	  object Custom {
159	
160	    /** Use a user-provided formatter for each rendered value. */
161	    def apply(formatValue: Value => String): Type =
162	      customType(failure => renderValues(failure)(formatValue))
163	
164	    /** Use a user-provided formatter for the failure message. */
165	    def apply(buildMessage: (Value, Value) => String): Type = message(buildMessage)
166	
167	    /** Use a user-provided formatter for the failure message while keeping the rendered values. */
168	    def message(buildMessage: (Value, Value) => String): Type = message(Dec)(buildMessage)
169	
170	    /** Use a user-provided formatter for the failure message on top of a base value format. */
171	    def message(base: Type)(buildMessage: (Value, Value) => String): Type =
172	      customType { failure =>
173	        val rendered = base.render(failure)
174	        rendered.copy(message = buildMessage(failure.observed, failure.expected))
175	      }
176	
177	    private def customType(renderFailure: Failure => Rendered): Type = new CustomType(renderFailure)
178	  }
179	
180	  private final class CustomType(renderFailure: Failure => Rendered) extends Type {
181	    override private[simulator] def render(failure: Failure): Rendered = renderFailure(failure)
182	  }
183	
184	  private def renderValues(failure: Failure)(formatValue: Value => String): Rendered = {
185	    val observed = formatValue(failure.observed)
186	    val expected = formatValue(failure.expected)
187	    Rendered(observed, expected, defaultMessage(observed, expected))
188	  }
189	
190	  private def defaultMessage(observed: String, expected: String): String =
191	    s"Expectation failed: observed value $observed != $expected"
192	
193	  private def formatGroupedHexDigits(value: Value): String = {
194	    val minimumDigits = if (value.bitWidth > 0) (value.bitWidth + 3) / 4 else 0
195	    groupDigits(padLeft(value.unsignedValue.toString(16), minimumDigits), 2)
196	  }
197	
198	  private def formatGroupedBinaryDigits(value: Value): String = {
199	    val minimumDigits = if (value.bitWidth > 0) value.bitWidth else 0
200	    groupDigits(padLeft(value.unsignedValue.toString(2), minimumDigits), 4)
201	  }
202	
203	  private def padLeft(digits: String, minimumWidth: Int): String = {
204	    if (minimumWidth > digits.length) {
205	      ("0" * (minimumWidth - digits.length)) + digits
206	    } else {
207	      digits
208	    }
209	  }
210	
211	  private def groupDigits(digits: String, groupSize: Int): String =
212	    digits.reverse.grouped(groupSize).map(_.reverse).toSeq.reverse.mkString(" ")
213	
214	  private def withType(chiselType: String, value: String): String = {
215	    if (chiselType.nonEmpty) s"$chiselType($value)" else value
216	  }
217	}
218	
219	/**
220	  * Exception thrown when an expectation fails.
221	  *
222	  * @param observed the observed value
223	  * @param expected the expected value
224	  * @param message the message to display
225	  */
226	case class FailedExpectationException[T <: Serializable](observed: T, expected: T, message: String)
227	    extends RuntimeException(
228	      dramaticMessage(
229	        header = Some("Failed Expectation"),
230	        body = s"""|Observed value: '$observed'
231	                   |Expected value: '$expected'
232	                   |$message""".stripMargin
233	      )
234	    )
235	    with PeekPokeApiException
236	
237	object FailedExpectationException {
238	  def apply[T <: Serializable](
239	    observed:   T,
240	    expected:   T,
241	    message:    String,
242	    sourceInfo: SourceInfo
243	  ): FailedExpectationException[T] = {
244	    val extraContext =
245	      sourceInfo match {
246	        case sl: SourceLine =>
247	          ExceptionHelpers.getErrorLineInFile(Seq(), sl)
248	        case _ =>
249	          Seq()
250	      }
251	    val fullMessage = s"$message ${sourceInfo.makeMessage()}" +
252	      (if (extraContext.nonEmpty) s"\n${extraContext.mkString("\n")}" else "")
253	    new FailedExpectationException[T](observed, expected, fullMessage)
254	  }
255	}

257	case class UninitializedElementException(message: String)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo)
258	    extends RuntimeException(
259	      {
260	        val extraContext =
261	          sourceInfo match {
262	            case sl: SourceLine =>
263	              ExceptionHelpers.getErrorLineInFile(Seq(), sl)
264	            case _ =>
265	              Seq()
266	          }
267	        dramaticMessage(
268	          header = Some("Uninitialized Element"),
269	          body = s"$message ${sourceInfo.makeMessage()}" +
270	            (if (extraContext.nonEmpty) s"\n${extraContext.mkString("\n")}" else "")
271	        )
272	      }
273	    )
274	    with PeekPokeApiException

307	sealed trait TestableElement[T <: Element] extends PeekPokable[T] {
308	  protected def isSigned = false
309	
310	  private[simulator] def encode(width: Int, value: BigInt): T
311	
312	  private[simulator] final def encode(value: Simulation.Value): T = {
313	    encode(value.bitCount, value.asBigInt)
314	  }
315	
316	  final def peekValue(): Simulation.Value = {
317	    simulatedModule.willPeek()
318	    simulationPort.get(isSigned = isSigned)
319	  }
320	
321	  def peek()(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): T = encode(peekValue())
322	
323	  def poke(literal: T): Unit = poke(literal.litValue)
324	
325	  def poke(value: BigInt): Unit = {
326	    simulatedModule.willPoke()
327	    simulationPort.set(value)
328	  }
329	
330	  private[simulator] def check[U](checkFn: Simulation.Value => Unit): Unit = {
331	    simulatedModule.willPeek()
332	    simulationPort.check(isSigned = isSigned)(checkFn)
333	  }
334	
335	  private def portBitWidth: Int = if (data.widthKnown) data.getWidth else 0
336	
337	  private def dataTypeString: String = chiselTypeOf(data).toString
338	
339	  private def valueContext(
340	    chiselType:    String,
341	    signedValue:   BigInt,
342	    bitWidth:      Int,
343	    displayString: String
344	  ): ExpectationValueFormat.Value =
345	    ExpectationValueFormat.Value(
346	      chiselType = chiselType,
347	      signedValue = signedValue,
348	      bitWidth = bitWidth,
349	      isSigned = isSigned,
350	      displayString = displayString
351	    )
352	
353	  protected final def valueContextForLiteral(
354	    literal:     Data,
355	    signedValue: BigInt,
356	    bitWidth:    Int
357	  ): ExpectationValueFormat.Value =
358	    valueContext(chiselTypeOf(literal).toString, signedValue, bitWidth, literal.toString)
359	
360	  protected final def valueContextForObservedLiteral(observed: Simulation.Value): ExpectationValueFormat.Value = {
361	    val encoded = encode(observed)
362	    valueContextForLiteral(encoded, observed.asBigInt, observed.bitCount)
363	  }
364	
365	  protected final def valueContextForExpectedLiteral(
366	    expected:         T,
367	    observedBitWidth: Int
368	  ): ExpectationValueFormat.Value = {
369	    val effectiveBitWidth = if (portBitWidth > 0) portBitWidth else observedBitWidth
370	    valueContextForRawValue(expected.litValue, effectiveBitWidth)
371	  }
372	
373	  private def displayStringForRawValue(value: BigInt, bitWidth: Int): String = {
374	    try {
375	      encode(bitWidth, value).toString
376	    } catch {
377	      case _: Exception =>
378	        if (dataTypeString.nonEmpty) s"$dataTypeString(${value.toString})" else value.toString
379	    }
380	  }
381	
382	  protected final def valueContextForRawValue(value: BigInt, bitWidth: Int): ExpectationValueFormat.Value =
383	    valueContext(dataTypeString, value, bitWidth, displayStringForRawValue(value, bitWidth))
384	
385	  protected final def valueContextForRawValue(
386	    value:         BigInt,
387	    bitWidth:      Int,
388	    displayString: String
389	  ): ExpectationValueFormat.Value =
390	    valueContext(dataTypeString, value, bitWidth, displayString)
391	
392	  protected final def valueContextForObservedRaw(observed: Simulation.Value): ExpectationValueFormat.Value =
393	    valueContextForRawValue(observed.asBigInt, observed.bitCount)
394	
395	  protected final def valueContextForExpectedRaw(
396	    expected:         BigInt,
397	    observedBitWidth: Int
398	  ): ExpectationValueFormat.Value = {
399	    val effectiveBitWidth = if (portBitWidth > 0) portBitWidth else observedBitWidth
400	    valueContextForRawValue(expected, effectiveBitWidth)
401	  }
402	
403	  protected final def renderFailureWithFormat(
404	    observed: ExpectationValueFormat.Value,
405	    expected: ExpectationValueFormat.Value,
406	    format:   ExpectationValueFormat.Type
407	  ): ExpectationValueFormat.Rendered =
408	    format.render(ExpectationValueFormat.Failure(observed, expected))
409	
410	  protected final def rawFailureMessage(observed: BigInt, expected: BigInt): String =
411	    s"Expectation failed: observed value ${observed.toString} != ${expected.toString}"
412	
413	  protected final def expect[U](
414	    expected:      U,
415	    sameValue:     (Simulation.Value, U) => Boolean,
416	    renderFailure: (Simulation.Value, U) => ExpectationValueFormat.Rendered,
417	    buildMessage:  (Simulation.Value, U) => String,
418	    sourceInfo:    SourceInfo
419	  ): Unit = {
420	    check(observedValue =>
421	      if (!sameValue(observedValue, expected)) {
422	        val rendered = renderFailure(observedValue, expected)
423	        throw FailedExpectationException(
424	          rendered.observed,
425	          rendered.expected,
426	          buildMessage(observedValue, expected),
427	          sourceInfo
428	        )
429	      }
430	    )
431	  }
432	
433	  protected final def expect[U](
434	    expected:      U,
435	    sameValue:     (Simulation.Value, U) => Boolean,
436	    renderFailure: (Simulation.Value, U) => ExpectationValueFormat.Rendered,
437	    sourceInfo:    SourceInfo
438	  ): Unit = {
439	    check(observedValue =>
440	      if (!sameValue(observedValue, expected)) {
441	        val rendered = renderFailure(observedValue, expected)
442	        throw FailedExpectationException(
443	          rendered.observed,
444	          rendered.expected,
445	          rendered.message,
446	          sourceInfo
447	        )
448	      }
449	    )
450	  }
451	
452	  protected final def expect[U](
453	    expected:       U,
454	    sameValue:      (Simulation.Value, U) => Boolean,
455	    formatObserved: Simulation.Value => String,
456	    formatExpected: U => String,
457	    buildMessage:   (Simulation.Value, U) => String,
458	    sourceInfo:     SourceInfo
459	  ): Unit = {
460	    check(observedValue =>
461	      if (!sameValue(observedValue, expected)) {
462	        throw FailedExpectationException(
463	          formatObserved(observedValue),
464	          formatExpected(expected),
465	          buildMessage(observedValue, expected),
466	          sourceInfo
467	        )
468	      }
469	    )
470	  }
471	
472	  protected final def expect[U](
473	    expected:       U,
474	    sameValue:      (Simulation.Value, U) => Boolean,
475	    formatObserved: Simulation.Value => String,
476	    formatExpected: U => String,
477	    sourceInfo:     SourceInfo
478	  ): Unit = {
479	    check(observedValue =>
480	      if (!sameValue(observedValue, expected)) {
481	        val observed = formatObserved(observedValue)
482	        val expectedValue = formatExpected(expected)
483	        throw FailedExpectationException(
484	          observed,
485	          expectedValue,
486	          s"Expectation failed: observed value $observed != $expectedValue",
487	          sourceInfo
488	        )
489	      }
490	    )
491	  }
492	
493	  /**
494	  * Expect the value of a data port to be equal to the expected value.
495	  *
496	  * @param expected the expected value
497	  * @param buildMessage a function taking (observedValue: T, expectedValue: T) and returning a String message for the failure case
498	  * @throws FailedExpectationException if the observed value does not match the expected value
499	  */
500	  def expect(expected: T, buildMessage: (T, T) => String)(
501	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
502	  ): Unit =
503	    expect(expected, buildMessage, ExpectationValueFormat.Dec)
504	
505	  def expect(
506	    expected:     T,
507	    buildMessage: (T, T) => String,
508	    format:       ExpectationValueFormat.Type
509	  )(
510	    implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo
511	  ): Unit = {
512	    require(expected.isLit, s"Expected value: $expected must be a literal")
513	    expect[T](
514	      expected,
515	      (observed: Simulation.Value, expected: T) => observed.asBigInt == expected.litValue,
516	      renderFailure = (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: T) =>
517	        renderFailureWithFormat(
518	          valueContextForObservedLiteral(obs),
519	          valueContextForExpectedLiteral(exp, obs.bitCount),
520	          format
521	        ),
522	      buildMessage = (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: T) => buildMessage(encode(obs), exp),
523	      sourceInfo = sourceInfo
524	    )
525	  }
526	
527	  override def expect(expected: T, message: String)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
528	    expect(expected, (_: T, _: T) => message, ExpectationValueFormat.Dec)
529	
530	  override def expect(
531	    expected: T,
532	    message:  String,
533	    format:   ExpectationValueFormat.Type
534	  )(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
535	    expect(expected, (_: T, _: T) => message, format)
536	
537	  final def expect(expected: BigInt)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
538	    expect[BigInt](
539	      expected,
540	      (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: BigInt) => obs.asBigInt == exp,
541	      renderFailure = (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: BigInt) => {
542	        val rendered = renderFailureWithFormat(
543	          valueContextForObservedRaw(obs),
544	          valueContextForExpectedRaw(exp, obs.bitCount),
545	          ExpectationValueFormat.Dec
546	        )
547	        rendered.copy(message = rawFailureMessage(obs.asBigInt, exp))
548	      },
549	      sourceInfo = sourceInfo
550	    )
551	
552	  final def expect(expected: BigInt, format: ExpectationValueFormat.Type)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
553	    expect[BigInt](
554	      expected,
555	      (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: BigInt) => obs.asBigInt == exp,
556	      renderFailure = (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: BigInt) =>
557	        renderFailureWithFormat(valueContextForObservedRaw(obs), valueContextForExpectedRaw(exp, obs.bitCount), format),
558	      sourceInfo = sourceInfo
559	    )
560	
561	  final def expect(expected: BigInt, message: String)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
562	    expect(expected, message, ExpectationValueFormat.Dec)
563	
564	  final def expect(
565	    expected: BigInt,
566	    message:  String,
567	    format:   ExpectationValueFormat.Type
568	  )(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit =
569	    expect[BigInt](
570	      expected,
571	      (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: BigInt) => obs.asBigInt == exp,
572	      renderFailure = (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: BigInt) =>
573	        renderFailureWithFormat(valueContextForObservedRaw(obs), valueContextForExpectedRaw(exp, obs.bitCount), format),
574	      buildMessage = (_: Simulation.Value, _: BigInt) => message,
575	      sourceInfo = sourceInfo
576	    )
577	
578	  override def expect(expected: T, format: ExpectationValueFormat.Type)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit = {
579	    require(expected.isLit, s"Expected value: $expected must be a literal")
580	    expect(
581	      expected,
582	      (observed: Simulation.Value, expected: T) => observed.asBigInt == expected.litValue,
583	      renderFailure = (obs: Simulation.Value, exp: T) =>
584	        renderFailureWithFormat(
585	          valueContextForObservedLiteral(obs),
586	          valueContextForExpectedLiteral(exp, obs.bitCount),
587	          format
588	        ),
589	      sourceInfo = sourceInfo
590	    )
591	  }
592	
593	  override def expect(expected: T)(implicit sourceInfo: SourceInfo): Unit = expect(expected, ExpectationValueFormat.Dec)
594	}
